#### Individual Curves Across Seasons, Stratified by Sex

```{r }
# Determine the prediction date range
date_seq <- seq(min(dat_sex$date_J_scaled), max(dat_sex$date_J_scaled), length.out = 100)

# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex <- dat_sex %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex)

# Expand grid for predictions
newdata <- expand_grid(
  date_J_scaled = date_seq,
  id_season_sex
)

# Get predicted curves with uncertainty
preds <- add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0, newdata = newdata, ndraws = 50)  # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves

preds %>%
  filter(is.na(date_J_scaled) | is.na(.epred) | is.na(rings_comb))

# faceted by individual
ggplot(preds, 
       aes(x = date_J_scaled, y = .epred, color = sex,
           group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.1) +
  facet_wrap(rings_comb ~ .) +
  labs(title = "Individual curves across seasons by sex", y = "Predicted score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))

ggplot(preds, 
       aes(x = date_J_scaled, y = .epred, color = sex,
           group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.01) +
  # facet_wrap(rings_comb ~ .) +
  labs(title = "Individual curves across seasons by sex", 
       y = "Predicted score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))

preds %>% ungroup() %>% filter(rings_comb == "RRGO") %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct()
```

### Bayesian non-linear mixed model of sex-specific pre-basic moult dynamics (timing constant)

```{r brms, eval = FALSE}
# Clean data
dat_out_rm <- dat %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>%
  # subset to columns of interest
  select(score, date_J, rings_comb_simp, sex, season) %>% 
  rename(rings_comb = rings_comb_simp) %>% 
  # reduce to distinct data
  distinct() %>%
  # classify score and sex
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(score),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
    rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb)
  ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  # drop NAs
  drop_na(score, date_J, rings_comb, sex, season) %>% 
  # convert to dataframe
  as.data.frame()

# Formula specification
brms_formula_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 <- bf(
  score ~ 1 + (baseline - 1) * (1 - 1 / (1 + exp(-k * (date_J_scaled - t0)))),
  baseline ~ sex + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  t0 ~ 1 + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  # not enough temporal coverage within individuals over the moult to reliably 
  # estimate the 'quickness' of the moult, so better to simply assese the intercept
  k ~ 1,
  nl = TRUE
)

# Prior specification
priors <- c(
  # informative prior for the baseline:
  # centered around 5.5 with some uncertainty (SD = 1)
  # lb = 0 ensures that the baseline cannot be negative, which makes biological sense
  prior(normal(5.5, 1), nlpar = "baseline", lb = 0),
  
  # weakly informative (over ~1 month range: ~day 109 to ~165), helping 
  # convergence and avoiding unrealistic late- or early-season inflection points 
  # without being too restrictive 
  prior(normal(0, 0.5), nlpar = "t0"),
  
  # weakly informative prior reflecting a belief that the transition is not 
  # instantaneous (very high k) or flat (very low k), but somewhere in the middle.
  # The SD covers a reasonable range of transition steepness given the temporal scale.
  # lb = 0 ensures a positive slope, avoiding biologically implausible inverse dynamics 
  # but note that the non-linear function's formula has -k, so it will be forced to a
  # negative slope.
  prior(normal(1, 0.5), nlpar = "k", lb = 0),
  
  # These define group-level standard deviations (variability) for the baseline 
  # and t0 parameters across individuals (rings_comb) and individuals nested within 
  # season (rings_comb:season)
  # exponential(2) is a weakly regularizing prior that favors smaller variation 
  # but still allows for larger SDs if supported by the data.
  # The exponential distribution has a mode at 0 but a long right tail, which 
  # makes it suitable when you expect many individuals to vary only modestly, 
  # but allow for occasional larger deviations.
  # These are standard weakly informative priors for hierarchical models where 
  # you expect some among-individual or within-individual-over-season variation, 
  # but want to discourage extreme variance unless strongly supported.
  # Using the same prior across groups ensures comparability and avoids unintentional 
  # weighting of one grouping level.
  # They are compatible with the principle of regularization, which improves 
  # model stability and predictive performance.
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season")
)

# Fit model
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 <- brm(
  formula = brms_formula_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0,
  data = dat_out_rm,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999, max_treedepth = 15),
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  iter = 10000,
  warmup = 5000,
  backend = "cmdstanr"
)

# write output to disk
saveRDS(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0, file = here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2.rds"))
```

Random-effects structure and variance partitioning

The variance components are nearly identical to the previous model.

Baseline

Among individuals: SD ≈ 0.53

Within individual × season: SD ≈ 0.38

Interpretation unchanged:

Strong among-individual repeatability

Meaningful seasonal plasticity

t0

Among individuals: SD ≈ 0.10 (CI touches 0)

Within individual × season: SD ≈ 0.34

Again, this reinforces:

Weak or absent repeatable individual differences in timing

Strong within-individual, across-season variation

Removing sex from the fixed-effects part of t0 does not alter this
variance structure, which strengthens confidence that the random-effects
specification is capturing the dominant signal.

```{r}
# ================================
# 1. Load model and inspect
# ================================
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 <- readRDS(
  here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2.rds")
)

# Model summary and diagnostics
summary(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)
plot(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)
variables(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)

# ================================
# 2. Extract posterior draws
# ================================
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)

# ----------------
# Standardized sex effect on baseline
resid_sd <- sd(dat_out_rm$score, na.rm = TRUE)
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd
age_class_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_age / resid_sd

# ----------------
# Compute sex-specific baseline medians and 95% CI
baseline_sex_summary <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_F = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_M = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    median_F     = median(baseline_F),
    ci_lower_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.025),
    ci_upper_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.975),
    median_M     = median(baseline_M),
    ci_lower_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.025),
    ci_upper_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.975)
  )

baseline_sex_summary

# ================================
# 3. Back-transform t0 and baseline
# ================================
date_scaling <- dat_out_rm %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_J = mean(date_J),
    sd_J   = sd(date_J),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mean_date_sd   <- mean(date_scaling$sd_J)
mean_date_mean <- mean(date_scaling$mean_J)

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # sex-specific baseline
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,

    # t0 is NOT sex-specific in this model
    t0_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0        = t0_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean
  )

# ================================
# 4. Posterior summary helper
# ================================
summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    median   = median(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# ----------------
# Summarize key fixed effects
std_sex_baseline_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_baseline_std)
t0_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$t0)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
t0_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))

t0_summary_trans

# ================================
# 5. Interpret k: peak rate of change in days·score⁻¹
# ================================
date_sd <- mean_date_sd

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # Peak rate in scores·day⁻¹
    rate_scores_day_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_scores_day_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    # Convert to days·score⁻¹
    rate_days_score_sexF = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexF,
    rate_days_score_sexM = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexM
  )

rate_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexF)
rate_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexM)

# ================================
# 6. Bayesian R²
# ================================
r2_df <- r2_bayes(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_summary <- tibble(
  type     = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
  mean     = c(r2_df$R2[2], r2_df$R2[1]),
  ci_lower = c(r2_df$CI_low[2], r2_df$CI_low[1]),
  ci_upper = c(r2_df$CI_high[2], r2_df$CI_high[1])
)

# ================================
# 7. Variance components & repeatabilities
# ================================
post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    var_baseline_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2,
    var_t0_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2,
    R_baseline = var_baseline_indiv / (var_baseline_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_t0       = var_t0_indiv / (var_t0_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_baseline_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2),
    R_t0_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2)
  )

R_baseline_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline)
R_t0_summary                     <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0)
R_baseline_within_season_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline_within_season)
R_t0_within_season_summary       <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0_within_season)

# ================================
# 8. Variance component table
# ================================
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2
)

var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2
)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

tibble(
  parameter = c(
    "Std. effect size (sex) on baseline",
    "Inflection point (t0)",
    "Median baseline (F)",
    "Median baseline (M)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, F)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, M)",
    "Marginal R²",
    "Conditional R²",
    "Random effect variance (baseline)",
    "Random effect variance (t0)",
    "Residual variance"
  ),
  mean = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["median"],
    t0_summary["median"],
    baseline_sex_summary$median_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$median_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["median"],
    rate_sexM_summary["median"],
    r2_summary$mean[1],
    r2_summary$mean[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["median"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["median"],
    resid_var["median"]
  ),
  ci_lower = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_lower"],
    t0_summary["ci_lower"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_lower"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_lower"],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[1],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    resid_var["ci_lower"]
  ),
  ci_upper = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_upper"],
    t0_summary["ci_upper"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_upper"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_upper"],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[1],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    resid_var["ci_upper"]
  )
)

# ================================
# 9. Posterior distributions of fixed effects
# ================================
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(
    b_baseline_Intercept,
    b_baseline_sexM,
    b_t0_Intercept,
    b_k_Intercept
  )

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>%
  select(-c(.chain, .iteration, .draw)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),
               fill = "skyblue",
               color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
    x = "Estimate",
    y = "Parameter"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 10. Access key derived quantities
# ================================
post$baseline_sexF
post$baseline_sexM
post$t0
post$rate_days_score_sexF
post$rate_days_score_sexM

# ================================
# 11. Posterior correlation between baseline and t0
# ================================
# Use the updated model with correlated random effect
draws_df <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# Check for correlation parameters
names(draws_df)[grepl("cor_rings_comb", names(draws_df))]
# Should return: "cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept"

# Extract posterior draws
cor_baseline_t0_post <- draws_df$cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept

# Posterior summary
cor_baseline_t0_summary <- c(
  median   = median(cor_baseline_t0_post),
  ci_lower = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.025),
  ci_upper = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.975)
)

cor_baseline_t0_summary

# Optional: visualize posterior correlation
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data.frame(cor = cor_baseline_t0_post), aes(x = cor)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(cor_baseline_t0_post), color = "darkblue", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Correlation between baseline and t0 (individual level)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Posterior distribution of correlation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 12. Posterior predictive checks
# ================================
library(brms)
# Plot observed vs simulated data
pp_check(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, ndraws = 100)

# Optional: numeric summaries for PPC
pp_draws <- posterior_predict(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, draws = 1000)
pp_summary <- apply(pp_draws, 2, function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sd = sd(x)))
head(pp_summary)

# ================================
# 13. R-hat checks for convergence
# ================================
rhat_values <- rhat(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)
summary(rhat_values)  # All should be ~1 for good convergence
```

```{r}
# bring the output into the R environment
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_.rds"))

# check model summary
summary(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)

# model diagnostics: check chains and posterior distributions
plot(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)
```

#### overall effects - sex

```{r}
# Extract posterior draws
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0)

# estimate SD of the response from posterior predictive simulations or observed data
resid_sd <- sd(dat_sex$score, na.rm = TRUE)

# Standardized baseline effect size
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd

# back-transform the t0 effect size
# Extract scaling parameters from your original scaling
date_J_mean <- attr(scale(dat_out_rm$date_J), "scaled:center")
date_J_sd   <- attr(scale(dat_out_rm$date_J), "scaled:scale")

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    t0_sexF_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_sexM_scaled = b_t0_Intercept + b_t0_sexM,
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM) %>% 
  mutate(
    t0_sexF = t0_sexF_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean,
    t0_sexM = t0_sexM_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean
  )

# Summarize the posterior distributions on original scale:
# Summarize
sex_effect_summary <- function(x) {
  c(mean = median(x), ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025), ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975))
}

std_sex_baseline <- sex_effect_summary(sex_baseline_std)
std_sex_t0 <- sex_effect_summary(post$b_t0_sexM)
summary_t0_sexF <- sex_effect_summary(post$t0_sexF)
summary_t0_sexM <- sex_effect_summary(post$t0_sexM)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
summary_t0_sexF_trans <- as.Date(summary_t0_sexF - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))
summary_t0_sexM_trans <- as.Date(summary_t0_sexM - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))

summary_t0_sexF_trans
summary_t0_sexM_trans

# interpretation of k-intercept:
date_sd <- dat_sex %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(sd = sd(date_J)) %>%
  summarise(mean_sd = mean(sd)) %>%
  pull(mean_sd)

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,
    
    # peak rate at t0
    rate_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd)
  )

rate_summary <- function(x) {
  c(
    median = median(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

rate_sexF_summary <- rate_summary(post$rate_sexF)
rate_sexM_summary <- rate_summary(post$rate_sexM)

# Total R²
r2_both <- 
  r2_bayes(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_both <- 
  tibble(
    type = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
    mean = c(r2_both$R2[2], r2_both$R2[1]),
    ci_lower = c(r2_both$CI_low[2], r2_both$CI_low[1]),
    ci_upper = c(r2_both$CI_high[2], r2_both$CI_high[1])
  )

summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    mean = mean(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# Baseline variance (rings_comb)
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2)

# t0 variance (rings_comb)
var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

tibble::tibble(
  parameter = c("Std. effect size (sex) on baseline",
                "Std. effect size (sex) on t0",
                "Marginal R²", 
                "Conditional R²",
                "Random effect variance (baseline)",
                "Random effect variance (t0)",
                "Residual variance"),
  mean = c(std_sex_baseline["mean"], std_sex_t0["mean"],
           r2_both$mean[1], r2_both$mean[2],
           var_baseline_rings_comb["mean"], var_t0_rings_comb["mean"],
           resid_var["mean"]),
  ci_lower = c(std_sex_baseline["ci_lower.2.5%"], std_sex_t0["ci_lower.2.5%"],
           r2_both$ci_lower[1], r2_both$ci_lower[2],
           var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower.2.5%"], var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower.2.5%"],
           resid_var["ci_lower.2.5%"]),
  ci_upper = c(std_sex_baseline["ci_upper.97.5%"], std_sex_t0["ci_upper.97.5%"],
           r2_both$ci_upper[1], r2_both$ci_upper[2],
           var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper.97.5%"], var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper.97.5%"],
           resid_var["ci_upper.97.5%"])
)

# Extract only fixed effects
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(b_baseline_Intercept, b_baseline_sexM,
               b_t0_Intercept, b_t0_sexM,
               b_k_Intercept)

# convert to long format
posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>% select(-c(`.chain`, `.iteration`, `.draw`)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

# plot the posteriors
ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), fill = "skyblue", color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
       x = "Estimate", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal()

# back-transformed sex-specific posterior distributions of pre-basic moult timing (t0)
post$t0_sexF
post$t0_sexM

# back-transformed sex-specific posterior distributions of pre-basic moult starting score (baseline)
post$baseline_sexF
post$baseline_sexM
```

#### individual posterior distributions

```{r}
# baseline
draws_long_baseline <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(`r_rings_comb:season__baseline`[level, param]) %>%
  rename(ring_season = level,
         baseline = `r_rings_comb:season__baseline`) %>%
  mutate(season = str_sub(ring_season, nchar(ring_season) - 3, nchar(ring_season)),
         rings_comb = str_sub(ring_season, 1, 4))

baseline_ranks_M <- 
  draws_long_baseline %>% 
  left_join(., dat_out_rm %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct(), relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(sex == "M") %>% 
  group_by(rings_comb) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(baseline), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rings_comb_ordered = factor(rings_comb, levels = rings_comb[order(rank)])) %>%
  select(rings_comb, rings_comb_ordered)

baseline_ranks_F <- 
  draws_long_baseline %>% 
  left_join(., dat_out_rm %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct(), relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(sex == "F") %>% 
  group_by(rings_comb) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(baseline), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rings_comb_ordered = factor(rings_comb, levels = rings_comb[order(rank)])) %>%
  select(rings_comb, rings_comb_ordered)

draws_long_baseline_ranked_F <- 
  baseline_ranks_F %>%
  left_join(draws_long_baseline)

draws_long_baseline_ranked_M <- 
  baseline_ranks_M %>%
  left_join(draws_long_baseline)

# plotting
male_baseline <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(data = draws_long_baseline_ranked_M, 
                      aes(x = baseline, y = rings_comb_ordered, 
                          group = ring_season), 
                      fill = "#4E79A7", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-2.5, 2.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.2) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 0, 5.5),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
  ylab("Male identity")

female_baseline <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(data = draws_long_baseline_ranked_F, 
                      aes(x = baseline, y = rings_comb_ordered, 
                          group = ring_season), 
                      fill = "#F28E2B", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-2.5, 2.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.2) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.margin = margin(0, 5.5, 5.5, 5.5),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.ticks.x.length = unit(0.2, "cm"),
        panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
  ylab("Female identity")

n_F <- draws_long_baseline_ranked_F %>% pull(rings_comb) %>% unique() %>% length()
n_M <- draws_long_baseline_ranked_M %>% pull(rings_comb) %>% unique() %>% length()

baseline_plot <- 
  male_baseline / female_baseline + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(1 * (n_M/(n_M+n_F)), 1 * (n_F/(n_M+n_F))))

# t0
draws_long_t0 <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(`r_rings_comb:season__t0`[level, param]) %>%
  rename(ring_season = level,
         t0 = `r_rings_comb:season__t0`) %>%
  mutate(season = str_sub(ring_season, nchar(ring_season) - 3, nchar(ring_season)),
         rings_comb = str_sub(ring_season, 1, 4))

t0_ranks_M <- 
  draws_long_t0 %>% 
  left_join(., dat_out_rm %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct(), relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(sex == "M") %>% 
  group_by(rings_comb) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(t0), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rings_comb_ordered = factor(rings_comb, levels = rings_comb[order(rank)])) %>%
  select(rings_comb, rings_comb_ordered)

t0_ranks_F <- 
  draws_long_t0 %>% 
  left_join(., dat_out_rm %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct(), relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(sex == "F") %>% 
  group_by(rings_comb) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(t0), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rings_comb_ordered = factor(rings_comb, levels = rings_comb[order(rank)])) %>%
  select(rings_comb, rings_comb_ordered)

draws_long_t0_ranked_F <- 
  t0_ranks_F %>%
  left_join(draws_long_t0)

draws_long_t0_ranked_M <- 
  t0_ranks_M %>%
  left_join(draws_long_t0)

# plotting
male_t0 <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(data = draws_long_t0_ranked_M, 
                      aes(x = t0, y = rings_comb_ordered, 
                          group = ring_season), 
                      fill = "#4E79A7", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1.5, 1.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.2) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 0, 5.5),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank())

female_t0 <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(data = draws_long_t0_ranked_F, 
                      aes(x = t0, y = rings_comb_ordered, 
                          group = ring_season), 
                      fill = "#F28E2B", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1.5, 1.5)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.margin = margin(0, 5.5, 5.5, 5.5),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.ticks.x.length = unit(0.2, "cm"),
        panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.2)

n_F <- draws_long_t0_ranked_F %>% pull(rings_comb) %>% unique() %>% length()
n_M <- draws_long_t0_ranked_M %>% pull(rings_comb) %>% unique() %>% length()

t0_plot <- 
  male_t0 / female_t0 + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(1 * (n_M/(n_M+n_F)), 1 * (n_F/(n_M+n_F))))

# combined plot
baseline_plot | t0_plot
```

#### Individual Curves Across Seasons, Stratified by Sex

```{r }
# Determine the prediction date range
date_seq <- seq(min(dat_out_rm$date_J_scaled), max(dat_out_rm$date_J_scaled), length.out = 100)

# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex <- dat_out_rm %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex)

# Expand grid for predictions
newdata <- expand_grid(
  date_J_scaled = date_seq,
  id_season_sex
)

# Get predicted curves with uncertainty
preds <- add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0, newdata = newdata, ndraws = 50)  # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves

preds %>%
  filter(is.na(date_J_scaled) | is.na(.epred) | is.na(rings_comb))

# faceted by individual
ggplot(preds, 
       aes(x = date_J_scaled, y = .epred, color = sex,
           group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.1) +
  facet_wrap(rings_comb ~ .) +
  labs(title = "Individual curves across seasons by sex", y = "Predicted score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))

ggplot(preds, 
       aes(x = date_J_scaled, y = .epred, color = sex,
           group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.01) +
  # facet_wrap(rings_comb ~ .) +
  labs(title = "Individual curves across seasons by sex", 
       y = "Predicted score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))
```

### Bayesian non-linear mixed model of age-specific pre-basic moult dynamics

```{r brms, eval = FALSE}
# Clean data
dat_age <- dat %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>%
  # subset to columns of interest
  select(score, date_J, rings_comb_simp, sex, season, age) %>%
  rename(rings_comb = rings_comb_simp) %>% 
  # reduce to distinct data
  distinct() %>%
  # classify score and sex
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(score),
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
    rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb)
  ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  # drop NAs
  drop_na(score, date_J, sex, age, rings_comb, season) %>% 
  # reduce to distinct data
  distinct() %>%
  # convert to dataframe
  as.data.frame() %>%
  # center age
  mutate(age_centered = age - mean(age, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  mutate(age_2plus = ifelse(age == 1, NA, age)) %>% 
  mutate(age_2plus_centered = age_2plus - mean(age, na.rm = TRUE))

# Formula specification
brms_formula_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0 <- bf(
  score ~ 1 + (baseline - 1) * (1 - 1 / (1 + exp(-k * (date_J_scaled - t0)))),
  baseline ~ sex + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  t0 ~ age_centered + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  # not enough temporal coverage within individuals over the moult to reliably 
  # estimate the 'quickness' of the moult, so better to simply assese the intercept
  k ~ 1,
  nl = TRUE
)

# Prior specification
priors <- c(
  # informative prior for the baseline:
  # centered around 5.5 with some uncertainty (SD = 1)
  # lb = 0 ensures that the baseline cannot be negative, which makes biological sense
  prior(normal(5.5, 1), nlpar = "baseline", lb = 0),
  
  # weakly informative (over ~1 month range: ~day 109 to ~165), helping 
  # convergence and avoiding unrealistic late- or early-season inflection points 
  # without being too restrictive 
  prior(normal(0, 0.5), nlpar = "t0"),
  
  # weakly informative prior reflecting a belief that the transition is not 
  # instantaneous (very high k) or flat (very low k), but somewhere in the middle.
  # The SD covers a reasonable range of transition steepness given the temporal scale.
  # lb = 0 ensures a positive slope, avoiding biologically implausible inverse dynamics 
  # but note that the non-linear function's formula has -k, so it will be forced to a
  # negative slope.
  prior(normal(1, 0.5), nlpar = "k", lb = 0),
  
  # These define group-level standard deviations (variability) for the baseline 
  # and t0 parameters across individuals (rings_comb) and individuals nested within 
  # season (rings_comb:season)
  # exponential(2) is a weakly regularizing prior that favors smaller variation 
  # but still allows for larger SDs if supported by the data.
  # The exponential distribution has a mode at 0 but a long right tail, which 
  # makes it suitable when you expect many individuals to vary only modestly, 
  # but allow for occasional larger deviations.
  # These are standard weakly informative priors for hierarchical models where 
  # you expect some among-individual or within-individual-over-season variation, 
  # but want to discourage extreme variance unless strongly supported.
  # Using the same prior across groups ensures comparability and avoids unintentional 
  # weighting of one grouping level.
  # They are compatible with the principle of regularization, which improves 
  # model stability and predictive performance.
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season")
)

# Fit model
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0 <- brm(
  formula = brms_formula_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0,
  data = dat_age %>% filter(!is.na(age_centered)),
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999, max_treedepth = 15),
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  iter = 10000,
  warmup = 5000,
  backend = "cmdstanr"
)

# write output to disk
saveRDS(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0, file = here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0_2.rds"))
```

```{r}
# bring the output into the R environment
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0.rds"))

# check model summary
summary(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0)

# model diagnostics: check chains and posterior distributions
plot(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0)
```

#### overall effects - age

```{r}
# Extract posterior draws
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0)

# Get mean and SD used for scaling date_J
date_J_mean <- attr(scale(dat_age$date_J), "scaled:center")
date_J_sd   <- attr(scale(dat_age$date_J), "scaled:scale")

# Create a vector of numeric ages to back-transform (all ages present in the dataset)
ages <- sort(unique(dat_age$age))

# Compute posterior t0 for each age
t0_post <- post %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("b_"), as.numeric)) %>% 
  # For each age, add age_centered * posterior slope to t0_Intercept
  { 
    df <- .
    t0_age_list <- lapply(ages, function(a) {
      age_centered <- a - mean(dat_age$age, na.rm = TRUE)
      t0_scaled <- df$b_t0_Intercept + df$b_t0_age_centered * age_centered
      # back-transform to original date scale
      t0_real <- t0_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean
      # summarize posterior
      tibble(
        age = a,
        mean = median(t0_real),
        ci_lower = quantile(t0_real, 0.025),
        ci_upper = quantile(t0_real, 0.975)
      )
    })
    bind_rows(t0_age_list)
  }

# Convert to real calendar dates if desired
t0_post <- t0_post %>%
  mutate(
    t0_date = as.Date(mean - 181 - 1, origin = "2021-01-01"),
    t0_date_lower = as.Date(ci_lower - 181 - 1, origin = "2021-01-01"),
    t0_date_upper = as.Date(ci_upper - 181 - 1, origin = "2021-01-01")
  )

t0_post
t0_post[c(1, 9) , c("age", "t0_date", "t0_date_lower", "t0_date_upper")]
```

```{r}
# Extract posterior draws
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0)

# Get SD used for scaling date_J
date_J_sd <- attr(scale(dat_age$date_J), "scaled:scale")

# Posterior slope for t0 ~ age_centered
t0_age_slope <- post$b_t0_age_centered

# Convert slope to days (multiply by SD of date_J)
t0_age_days <- t0_age_slope * date_J_sd

# Summarize as median and 95% credible interval
t0_age_days_summary <- tibble(
  mean = median(t0_age_days),
  ci_lower = quantile(t0_age_days, 0.025),
  ci_upper = quantile(t0_age_days, 0.975)
)

t0_age_days_summary
```

```{r}
variables(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0)

# Extract only fixed effects
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(b_baseline_Intercept, b_baseline_sexM,
               b_t0_Intercept, b_t0_age_centered,
               b_k_Intercept)

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>% select(-c(`.chain`, `.iteration`, `.draw`)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), fill = "skyblue", color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
       x = "Estimate", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal()
```

#### individual posterior distributions - age

```{r}
# baseline
draws_long_baseline <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(`r_rings_comb:season__baseline`[level, param]) %>%
  rename(ring_season = level,
         baseline = `r_rings_comb:season__baseline`) %>%
  mutate(season = str_sub(ring_season, nchar(ring_season) - 3, nchar(ring_season)),
         rings_comb = str_sub(ring_season, 1, 4))

baseline_ranks_M <- 
  draws_long_baseline %>% 
  left_join(., fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0$data %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct(), relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(sex == "M") %>% 
  group_by(rings_comb) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(baseline), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rings_comb_ordered = factor(rings_comb, levels = rings_comb[order(rank)])) %>%
  select(rings_comb, rings_comb_ordered)

baseline_ranks_F <- 
  draws_long_baseline %>% 
  left_join(., fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0$data %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct(), relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(sex == "F") %>% 
  group_by(rings_comb) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(baseline), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rings_comb_ordered = factor(rings_comb, levels = rings_comb[order(rank)])) %>%
  select(rings_comb, rings_comb_ordered)

draws_long_baseline_ranked_F <- 
  baseline_ranks_F %>%
  left_join(draws_long_baseline)

draws_long_baseline_ranked_M <- 
  baseline_ranks_M %>%
  left_join(draws_long_baseline)

# plotting
male_baseline <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(data = draws_long_baseline_ranked_M, 
                      aes(x = baseline, y = rings_comb_ordered, 
                          group = ring_season), 
                      fill = "#4E79A7", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-2.5, 2.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.2) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 0, 5.5),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
  ylab("Male identity")

female_baseline <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(data = draws_long_baseline_ranked_F, 
                      aes(x = baseline, y = rings_comb_ordered, 
                          group = ring_season), 
                      fill = "#F28E2B", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-2.5, 2.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.2) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.margin = margin(0, 5.5, 5.5, 5.5),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.ticks.x.length = unit(0.2, "cm"),
        panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
  ylab("Female identity")

n_F <- draws_long_baseline_ranked_F %>% pull(rings_comb) %>% unique() %>% length()
n_M <- draws_long_baseline_ranked_M %>% pull(rings_comb) %>% unique() %>% length()

baseline_plot <- 
  male_baseline / female_baseline + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(1 * (n_M/(n_M+n_F)), 1 * (n_F/(n_M+n_F))))

# t0
draws_long_t0 <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(`r_rings_comb:season__t0`[level, param]) %>%
  rename(ring_season = level,
         t0 = `r_rings_comb:season__t0`) %>%
  mutate(season = str_sub(ring_season, nchar(ring_season) - 3, nchar(ring_season)),

  group_by(age, rings_comb) %>% 
  summarise(t0_median = median(t0))

  theme_bw() +

        axis.title.x = element_blank(),

        plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 0, 5.5),

    facet_grid(age ~ .)
```

#### Individual Curves Across Seasons, Stratified by age

```{r }
# Determine the prediction date range
date_seq <- seq(min(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0$data$date_J_scaled), max(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0$data$date_J_scaled), length.out = 100)

# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_agec_t0$data %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex)

# Expand grid for predictions
newdata <- expand_grid(
  date_J_scaled = date_seq,
  id_season_sex
)

# Get predicted curves with uncertainty
preds <- add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0, newdata = newdata, ndraws = 50)  # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves

preds %>%
  filter(is.na(date_J_scaled) | is.na(.epred) | is.na(rings_comb))

ggplot(preds, 
       aes(x = date_J_scaled, y = .epred, color = sex,
           group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.1) +
  facet_wrap(rings_comb ~ .) +
  labs(title = "Individual curves across seasons by sex", y = "Predicted score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))

preds %>% ungroup() %>% filter(rings_comb == "RRGO") %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct()
```

### Bayesian non-linear mixed model of age-class-specific pre-basic moult dynamics

```{r brms, eval = FALSE}
# Clean data
dat_age_class <- dat %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>%
  # subset to columns of interest
  select(score, date_J, rings_comb_simp, sex, season, age) %>%
  rename(rings_comb = rings_comb_simp) %>% 
  # reduce to distinct data
  distinct() %>% 
  # classify score and sex
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(score),
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
    rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb),
    age_class = factor(ifelse(age == 1, "FY", "AFY"), levels = c("FY", "AFY"))
  ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  # drop NAs
  drop_na(score, date_J, sex, age, age_class, rings_comb, season) %>% 
  # convert to dataframe
  as.data.frame()

# see what the mean and SD are for date_J
scaler <- scale(dat_age$date_J)
attr(scaler, "scaled:center")  # mean
attr(scaler, "scaled:scale")   # SD

# Formula specification
brms_formula_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0 <- bf(
  score ~ 1 + (baseline - 1) * (1 - 1 / (1 + exp(-k * (date_J_scaled - t0)))),
  baseline ~ sex + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  t0 ~ age_class + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  # not enough temporal coverage within individuals over the moult to reliably 
  # estimate the 'quickness' of the moult, so better to simply assese the intercept
  k ~ 1,
  nl = TRUE
)

# Prior specification
priors <- c(
  # informative prior for the baseline:
  # centered around 5.5 with some uncertainty (SD = 1)
  # lb = 0 ensures that the baseline cannot be negative, which makes biological sense
  prior(normal(5.5, 1), nlpar = "baseline", lb = 0),
  
  # weakly informative (over ~1 month range: ~day 109 to ~165), helping 
  # convergence and avoiding unrealistic late- or early-season inflection points 
  # without being too restrictive 
  prior(normal(0, 0.5), nlpar = "t0"),
  
  # weakly informative prior reflecting a belief that the transition is not 
  # instantaneous (very high k) or flat (very low k), but somewhere in the middle.
  # The SD covers a reasonable range of transition steepness given the temporal scale.
  # lb = 0 ensures a positive slope, avoiding biologically implausible inverse dynamics 
  # but note that the non-linear function's formula has -k, so it will be forced to a
  # negative slope.
  prior(normal(1, 0.5), nlpar = "k", lb = 0),
  
  # These define group-level standard deviations (variability) for the baseline 
  # and t0 parameters across individuals (rings_comb) and individuals nested within 
  # season (rings_comb:season)
  # exponential(2) is a weakly regularizing prior that favors smaller variation 
  # but still allows for larger SDs if supported by the data.
  # The exponential distribution has a mode at 0 but a long right tail, which 
  # makes it suitable when you expect many individuals to vary only modestly, 
  # but allow for occasional larger deviations.
  # These are standard weakly informative priors for hierarchical models where 
  # you expect some among-individual or within-individual-over-season variation, 
  # but want to discourage extreme variance unless strongly supported.
  # Using the same prior across groups ensures comparability and avoids unintentional 
  # weighting of one grouping level.
  # They are compatible with the principle of regularization, which improves 
  # model stability and predictive performance.
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season")
)

# Fit model
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0 <- brm(
  formula = brms_formula_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0,
  data = dat_age_class,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999, max_treedepth = 15),
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  iter = 10000,
  warmup = 5000,
  backend = "cmdstanr"
)

# write output to  disk
saveRDS(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0, file = here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0_2.rds"))
```

```{r}
# bring the output into the R environment
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0_.rds"))

# check model summary
summary(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0)

# model diagnostics: check chains and posterior distributions
plot(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0)
```

#### overall effects - age class

```{r}
# Extract posterior draws
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0)

# estimate SD of the response from posterior predictive simulations or observed data
resid_sd <- sd(dat_age_class$score, na.rm = TRUE)

# Standardized baseline effect size
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd

# back-transform the t0 effect size
# Extract scaling parameters from your original scaling
date_J_mean <- attr(scale(dat_age_class$date_J), "scaled:center")
date_J_sd   <- attr(scale(dat_age_class$date_J), "scaled:scale")

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    t0_age_classFY_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_age_classAFY_scaled = b_t0_Intercept + b_t0_age_classAFY,
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM) %>% 
  mutate(
    t0_age_classFY = t0_age_classFY_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean,
    t0_age_classAFY = t0_age_classAFY_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean
  )

# Summarize the posterior distributions on original scale:
# Summarize
sex_effect_summary <- function(x) {
  c(mean = median(x), ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025), ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975))
}

std_sex_baseline <- sex_effect_summary(sex_baseline_std)
std_age_class_t0 <- sex_effect_summary(post$b_t0_age_classAFY)
summary_t0_age_classFY <- sex_effect_summary(post$t0_age_classFY)
summary_t0_age_classAFY <- sex_effect_summary(post$t0_age_classAFY)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
summary_t0_age_classFY_trans <- 
  as.Date(summary_t0_age_classFY - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))
summary_t0_age_classAFY_trans <- 
  as.Date(summary_t0_age_classAFY - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))

summary_t0_age_classFY_trans
summary_t0_age_classAFY_trans

# interpretation of k-intercept:
date_sd <- dat_sex %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(sd = sd(date_J)) %>%
  summarise(mean_sd = mean(sd)) %>%
  pull(mean_sd)

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,
    
    # peak rate at t0
    rate_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd)
  )

# Total R²
r2_both <- 
  r2_bayes(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_both <- 
  tibble(
    type = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
    mean = c(r2_both$R2[2], r2_both$R2[1]),
    ci_lower = c(r2_both$CI_low[2], r2_both$CI_low[1]),
    ci_upper = c(r2_both$CI_high[2], r2_both$CI_high[1])
  )

summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    mean = mean(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# Baseline variance (rings_comb)
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2)

# t0 variance (rings_comb)
var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

tibble::tibble(
  parameter = c("Std. effect size (sex) on baseline",
                "Std. effect size (sex) on t0",
                "Marginal R²", 
                "Conditional R²",
                "Random effect variance (baseline)",
                "Random effect variance (t0)",
                "Residual variance"),
  mean = c(std_sex_baseline["mean"], std_sex_t0["mean"],
           r2_both$mean[1], r2_both$mean[2],
           var_baseline_rings_comb["mean"], var_t0_rings_comb["mean"],
           resid_var["mean"]),
  ci_lower = c(std_sex_baseline["ci_lower.2.5%"], std_sex_t0["ci_lower.2.5%"],
           r2_both$ci_lower[1], r2_both$ci_lower[2],
           var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower.2.5%"], var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower.2.5%"],
           resid_var["ci_lower.2.5%"]),
  ci_upper = c(std_sex_baseline["ci_upper.97.5%"], std_sex_t0["ci_upper.97.5%"],
           r2_both$ci_upper[1], r2_both$ci_upper[2],
           var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper.97.5%"], var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper.97.5%"],
           resid_var["ci_upper.97.5%"])
)

# Extract only fixed effects
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(b_baseline_Intercept, b_baseline_sexM,
               b_t0_Intercept, b_t0_sexM,
               b_k_Intercept)

# convert to long format
posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>% select(-c(`.chain`, `.iteration`, `.draw`)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

# plot the posteriors
ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), fill = "skyblue", color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
       x = "Estimate", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal()

# back-transformed sex-specific posterior distributions of pre-basic moult timing (t0)
post$t0_sexF
post$t0_sexM

# back-transformed sex-specific posterior distributions of pre-basic moult starting score (baseline)
post$baseline_sexF
post$baseline_sexM
```

```{r}
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(b_baseline_Intercept, b_baseline_sexM,
               b_t0_Intercept, b_t0_age_classAFY,
               b_k_Intercept)

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>% select(-c(`.chain`, `.iteration`, `.draw`)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), fill = "skyblue", color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
       x = "Estimate", y = "Parameter") +
```

#### individual posterior distributions - age class

```{r}
# baseline
draws_long_baseline <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(`r_rings_comb:season__baseline`[level, param]) %>%
  rename(ring_season = level,
         baseline = `r_rings_comb:season__baseline`) %>%
  mutate(season = str_sub(ring_season, nchar(ring_season) - 3, nchar(ring_season)),
         rings_comb = str_sub(ring_season, 1, 4))

baseline_ranks_M <- 
  draws_long_baseline %>% 
  left_join(., fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0$data %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct(), relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(sex == "M") %>% 
  group_by(rings_comb) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(baseline), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rings_comb_ordered = factor(rings_comb, levels = rings_comb[order(rank)])) %>%
  select(rings_comb, rings_comb_ordered)

baseline_ranks_F <- 
  draws_long_baseline %>% 
  left_join(., fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0$data %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct(), relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(sex == "F") %>% 
  group_by(rings_comb) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(baseline), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rings_comb_ordered = factor(rings_comb, levels = rings_comb[order(rank)])) %>%
  select(rings_comb, rings_comb_ordered)

draws_long_baseline_ranked_F <- 
  baseline_ranks_F %>%
  left_join(draws_long_baseline)

draws_long_baseline_ranked_M <- 
  baseline_ranks_M %>%
  left_join(draws_long_baseline)

# plotting
male_baseline <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(data = draws_long_baseline_ranked_M, 
                      aes(x = baseline, y = rings_comb_ordered, 
                          group = ring_season), 
                      fill = "#4E79A7", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-2.5, 2.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.2) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 0, 5.5),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
  ylab("Male identity")

female_baseline <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(data = draws_long_baseline_ranked_F, 
                      aes(x = baseline, y = rings_comb_ordered, 
                          group = ring_season), 
                      fill = "#F28E2B", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-2.5, 2.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.2) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.margin = margin(0, 5.5, 5.5, 5.5),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.ticks.x.length = unit(0.2, "cm"),
        panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
  ylab("Female identity")

n_F <- draws_long_baseline_ranked_F %>% pull(rings_comb) %>% unique() %>% length()
n_M <- draws_long_baseline_ranked_M %>% pull(rings_comb) %>% unique() %>% length()

baseline_plot <- 
  male_baseline / female_baseline + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(1 * (n_M/(n_M+n_F)), 1 * (n_F/(n_M+n_F))))

# t0
draws_long_t0 <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(`r_rings_comb:season__t0`[level, param]) %>%
  rename(ring_season = level,
         t0 = `r_rings_comb:season__t0`) %>%
  mutate(season = str_sub(ring_season, nchar(ring_season) - 3, nchar(ring_season)),
         rings_comb = str_sub(ring_season, 1, 4))

t0_ranks_FY <- 
  draws_long_t0 %>% 
  left_join(., dat_age_class %>% select(rings_comb, age_class) %>% distinct(), relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(age_class == "FY") %>% 
  group_by(rings_comb) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(t0), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rings_comb_ordered = factor(rings_comb, levels = rings_comb[order(rank)])) %>%
  select(rings_comb, rings_comb_ordered)

t0_ranks_AFY <- 
  draws_long_t0 %>% 
  left_join(., dat_age_class %>% select(rings_comb, age_class) %>% distinct(), relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(age_class == "AFY") %>% 
  group_by(rings_comb) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(t0), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-mean_value)) %>%
  mutate(rings_comb_ordered = factor(rings_comb, levels = rings_comb[order(rank)])) %>%
  select(rings_comb, rings_comb_ordered)

draws_long_t0_ranked_FY <- 
  t0_ranks_FY %>%
  left_join(draws_long_t0)

draws_long_t0_ranked_AFY <- 
  t0_ranks_AFY %>%
  left_join(draws_long_t0)

# plotting
FY_t0 <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(data = draws_long_t0_ranked_FY, 
                      aes(x = t0, y = rings_comb_ordered, 
                          group = ring_season), 
                      fill = "#4E79A7", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1.5, 1.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.2) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 0, 5.5),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank())

AFY_t0 <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_density_ridges(data = draws_long_t0_ranked_AFY, 
                      aes(x = t0, y = rings_comb_ordered, 
                          group = ring_season), 
                      fill = "#F28E2B", color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1.5, 1.5)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.margin = margin(0, 5.5, 5.5, 5.5),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.ticks.x.length = unit(0.2, "cm"),
        panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.2)

n_FY <- draws_long_t0_ranked_FY %>% pull(rings_comb) %>% unique() %>% length()
n_AFY <- draws_long_t0_ranked_AFY %>% pull(rings_comb) %>% unique() %>% length()

t0_plot <- 
  FY_t0 / AFY_t0 + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(1 * (n_FY/(n_FY+n_AFY)), 1 * (n_AFY/(n_FY+n_AFY))))

# combined plot
baseline_plot | t0_plot
```

```{r }
date_seq <- seq(min(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0$data$date_J_scaled), max(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0$data$date_J_scaled), length.out = 100)

# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex_age_class <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0$data %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex, age_class)

# Expand grid for predictions
newdata <- expand_grid(

# Get predicted curves with uncertainty

preds %>%
```

### Bayesian non-linear mixed model of migratory status-specific pre-basic moult dynamics

```{r brms, eval = FALSE}
# Clean data
dat_migratory_status <- dat %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>%
  # subset to columns of interest
  select(score, date_J, rings_comb_simp, sex, season, migratory_status) %>%
  rename(rings_comb = rings_comb_simp) %>%
  # reduce to distinct data
  distinct() %>% 
  # remove blanck status
  filter(migratory_status %in% c("R", "M")) %>% 
  # classify score and sex
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(score),
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
    rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb),
    migratory_status = factor(migratory_status, levels = c("M", "R"))
  ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  # drop NAs
  drop_na(score, date_J, sex, migratory_status, rings_comb, season) %>% 
  # convert to dataframe
  as.data.frame()

# see what the mean and SD are for date_J
scaler <- scale(dat_migratory_status$date_J)
attr(scaler, "scaled:center")  # mean
attr(scaler, "scaled:scale")   # SD

# Formula specification
brms_formula_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0 <- bf(
  score ~ 1 + (baseline - 1) * (1 - 1 / (1 + exp(-k * (date_J_scaled - t0)))),
  baseline ~ sex + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  t0 ~ migratory_status + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  # not enough temporal coverage within individuals over the moult to reliably 
  # estimate the 'quickness' of the moult, so better to simply assese the intercept
  k ~ 1,
  nl = TRUE
)

# Prior specification
priors <- c(
  # informative prior for the baseline:
  # centered around 5.5 with some uncertainty (SD = 1)
  # lb = 0 ensures that the baseline cannot be negative, which makes biological sense
  prior(normal(5.5, 1), nlpar = "baseline", lb = 0),
  
  # weakly informative (over ~1 month range: ~day 109 to ~165), helping 
  # convergence and avoiding unrealistic late- or early-season inflection points 
  # without being too restrictive 
  prior(normal(0, 0.5), nlpar = "t0"),
  
  # weakly informative prior reflecting a belief that the transition is not 
  # instantaneous (very high k) or flat (very low k), but somewhere in the middle.
  # The SD covers a reasonable range of transition steepness given the temporal scale.
  # lb = 0 ensures a positive slope, avoiding biologically implausible inverse dynamics 
  # but note that the non-linear function's formula has -k, so it will be forced to a
  # negative slope.
  prior(normal(1, 0.5), nlpar = "k", lb = 0),
  
  # These define group-level standard deviations (variability) for the baseline 
  # and t0 parameters across individuals (rings_comb) and individuals nested within 
  # season (rings_comb:season)
  # exponential(2) is a weakly regularizing prior that favors smaller variation 
  # but still allows for larger SDs if supported by the data.
  # The exponential distribution has a mode at 0 but a long right tail, which 
  # makes it suitable when you expect many individuals to vary only modestly, 
  # but allow for occasional larger deviations.
  # These are standard weakly informative priors for hierarchical models where 
  # you expect some among-individual or within-individual-over-season variation, 
  # but want to discourage extreme variance unless strongly supported.
  # Using the same prior across groups ensures comparability and avoids unintentional 
  # weighting of one grouping level.
  # They are compatible with the principle of regularization, which improves 
  # model stability and predictive performance.
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season")
)

# Fit model
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0 <- brm(
  formula = brms_formula_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0,
  data = dat_migratory_status,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999, max_treedepth = 15),
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  iter = 10000,
  warmup = 5000,
  backend = "cmdstanr"
)

# write output to disk
saveRDS(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0, file = here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0_2.rds"))
```

```{r}
# bring the output into the R environment
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0.rds"))

# check model summary
summary(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0)

# model diagnostics: check chains and posterior distributions
plot(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0)
```

#### overall effects - migratory status

```{r}
# Extract posterior draws
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0)

# estimate SD of the response from posterior predictive simulations or observed data
resid_sd <- sd(dat_migratory_status$score, na.rm = TRUE)

# Standardized baseline effect size
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd

# back-transform the t0 effect size
# Extract scaling parameters from your original scaling
date_J_mean <- attr(scale(dat_migratory_status$date_J), "scaled:center")
date_J_sd   <- attr(scale(dat_migratory_status$date_J), "scaled:scale")

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    t0_migratory_statusM_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_migratory_statusR_scaled = b_t0_Intercept + b_t0_migratory_statusR,
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM) %>% 
  mutate(
    t0_migratory_statusM = t0_migratory_statusM_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean,
    t0_migratory_statusR = t0_migratory_statusR_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean
  )

# Summarize the posterior distributions on original scale:
# Summarize
sex_effect_summary <- function(x) {
  c(mean = median(x), ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025), ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975))
}

std_sex_baseline <- sex_effect_summary(sex_baseline_std)
std_migratory_status_t0 <- sex_effect_summary(post$b_t0_migratory_statusR)
summary_t0_migratory_statusM <- sex_effect_summary(post$t0_migratory_statusM)
summary_t0_migratory_statusR <- sex_effect_summary(post$t0_migratory_statusR)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
summary_t0_migratory_statusM_trans <- 
  as.Date(summary_t0_migratory_statusM - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))
summary_t0_migratory_statusR_trans <- 
  as.Date(summary_t0_migratory_statusR - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))

summary_t0_migratory_statusM_trans
summary_t0_migratory_statusR_trans

# interpretation of k-intercept:
date_sd <- dat_sex %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(sd = sd(date_J)) %>%
  summarise(mean_sd = mean(sd)) %>%
  pull(mean_sd)

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,
    
    # peak rate at t0
    rate_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd)
  )

```

```{r}
variables(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0)

# Extract only fixed effects
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(b_baseline_Intercept, b_baseline_sexM,
               b_t0_Intercept, b_t0_migratory_statusR,
               b_k_Intercept)

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>% select(-c(`.chain`, `.iteration`, `.draw`)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), fill = "skyblue", color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
       x = "Estimate", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal()
```
