### Age-class-specific timing and baseline

```{r brms, eval = FALSE}
# Clean data
dat_age_class <- dat %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>%
  # subset to columns of interest
  select(score, date_J, rings_comb_simp, sex, season, age) %>%
  rename(rings_comb = rings_comb_simp) %>% 
  # reduce to distinct data
  distinct() %>% 
  # classify score and sex
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(score),
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
    rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb),
    age_class = factor(ifelse(age == 1, "FY", "AFY"), levels = c("FY", "AFY"))
  ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  # drop NAs
  drop_na(score, date_J, sex, age, age_class, rings_comb, season) %>% 
  # convert to dataframe
  as.data.frame()

# see what the mean and SD are for date_J
scaler <- scale(dat_age$date_J)
attr(scaler, "scaled:center")  # mean
attr(scaler, "scaled:scale")   # SD

# Formula specification
brms_formula_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_sex_age_class_t0 <- bf(
  score ~ 1 + (baseline - 1) * (1 - 1 / (1 + exp(-k * (date_J_scaled - t0)))),
  baseline ~ sex + age_class + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  t0 ~ sex + age_class + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  # not enough temporal coverage within individuals over the moult to reliably 
  # estimate the 'quickness' of the moult, so better to simply assese the intercept
  k ~ 1,
  nl = TRUE
)

# Prior specification
priors <- c(
  # informative prior for the baseline:
  # centered around 5.5 with some uncertainty (SD = 1)
  # lb = 0 ensures that the baseline cannot be negative, which makes biological sense
  prior(normal(5.5, 1), nlpar = "baseline", lb = 0),
  
  # weakly informative (over ~1 month range: ~day 109 to ~165), helping 
  # convergence and avoiding unrealistic late- or early-season inflection points 
  # without being too restrictive 
  prior(normal(0, 0.5), nlpar = "t0"),
  
  # weakly informative prior reflecting a belief that the transition is not 
  # instantaneous (very high k) or flat (very low k), but somewhere in the middle.
  # The SD covers a reasonable range of transition steepness given the temporal scale.
  # lb = 0 ensures a positive slope, avoiding biologically implausible inverse dynamics 
  # but note that the non-linear function's formula has -k, so it will be forced to a
  # negative slope.
  prior(normal(1, 0.5), nlpar = "k", lb = 0),
  
  # These define group-level standard deviations (variability) for the baseline 
  # and t0 parameters across individuals (rings_comb) and individuals nested within 
  # season (rings_comb:season)
  # exponential(2) is a weakly regularizing prior that favors smaller variation 
  # but still allows for larger SDs if supported by the data.
  # The exponential distribution has a mode at 0 but a long right tail, which 
  # makes it suitable when you expect many individuals to vary only modestly, 
  # but allow for occasional larger deviations.
  # These are standard weakly informative priors for hierarchical models where 
  # you expect some among-individual or within-individual-over-season variation, 
  # but want to discourage extreme variance unless strongly supported.
  # Using the same prior across groups ensures comparability and avoids unintentional 
  # weighting of one grouping level.
  # They are compatible with the principle of regularization, which improves 
  # model stability and predictive performance.
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season")
)

# Fit model
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_sex_age_class_t0 <- brm(
  formula = brms_formula_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_sex_age_class_t0,
  data = dat_age_class,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999, max_treedepth = 15),
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  iter = 10000,
  warmup = 5000,
  backend = "cmdstanr"
)

# write output to  disk
saveRDS(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_sex_age_class_t0, file = here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_sex_age_class_t0_2.rds"))
```

```{r}
# ================================
# 1. Load model and inspect
# ================================
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_agec_baseline_agec_t0 <- readRDS(
  here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_agec_baseline_agec_t0_2.rds")
)

# Model summary and diagnostics
summary(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_t0)
plot(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)
variables(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)

# ================================
# 2. Extract posterior draws
# ================================
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# ----------------
# Standardized sex effect on baseline
resid_sd <- sd(dat_out_rm$score, na.rm = TRUE)
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd

# ----------------
# Compute sex-specific baseline medians and 95% CI
baseline_sex_summary <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_F = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_M = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    median_F     = median(baseline_F),
    ci_lower_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.025),
    ci_upper_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.975),
    median_M     = median(baseline_M),
    ci_lower_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.025),
    ci_upper_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.975)
  )

baseline_sex_summary

# ================================
# 3. Back-transform t0 and baseline
# ================================
date_scaling <- dat_out_rm %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_J = mean(date_J),
    sd_J   = sd(date_J),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mean_date_sd   <- mean(date_scaling$sd_J)
mean_date_mean <- mean(date_scaling$mean_J)

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # sex-specific baseline
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,

    # t0 is NOT sex-specific in this model
    t0_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0        = t0_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean
  )

# ================================
# 4. Posterior summary helper
# ================================
summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    median   = median(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# ----------------
# Summarize key fixed effects
std_sex_baseline_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_baseline_std)
t0_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$t0)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
t0_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))

t0_summary_trans

# ================================
# 5. Interpret k: peak rate of change in days·score⁻¹
# ================================
date_sd <- mean_date_sd

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # Peak rate in scores·day⁻¹
    rate_scores_day_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_scores_day_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    # Convert to days·score⁻¹
    rate_days_score_sexF = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexF,
    rate_days_score_sexM = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexM
  )

rate_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexF)
rate_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexM)

# ================================
# 6. Bayesian R²
# ================================
r2_df <- r2_bayes(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_summary <- tibble(
  type     = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
  mean     = c(r2_df$R2[2], r2_df$R2[1]),
  ci_lower = c(r2_df$CI_low[2], r2_df$CI_low[1]),
  ci_upper = c(r2_df$CI_high[2], r2_df$CI_high[1])
)

# ================================
# 7. Variance components & repeatabilities
# ================================
post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    var_baseline_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2,
    var_t0_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2,
    R_baseline = var_baseline_indiv / (var_baseline_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_t0       = var_t0_indiv / (var_t0_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_baseline_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2),
    R_t0_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2)
  )

R_baseline_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline)
R_t0_summary                     <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0)
R_baseline_within_season_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline_within_season)
R_t0_within_season_summary       <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0_within_season)

# ================================
# 8. Variance component table
# ================================
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2
)

var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2
)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

tibble(
  parameter = c(
    "Std. effect size (sex) on baseline",
    "Inflection point (t0)",
    "Median baseline (F)",
    "Median baseline (M)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, F)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, M)",
    "Marginal R²",
    "Conditional R²",
    "Random effect variance (baseline)",
    "Random effect variance (t0)",
    "Residual variance"
  ),
  mean = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["median"],
    t0_summary["median"],
    baseline_sex_summary$median_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$median_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["median"],
    rate_sexM_summary["median"],
    r2_summary$mean[1],
    r2_summary$mean[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["median"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["median"],
    resid_var["median"]
  ),
  ci_lower = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_lower"],
    t0_summary["ci_lower"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_lower"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_lower"],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[1],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    resid_var["ci_lower"]
  ),
  ci_upper = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_upper"],
    t0_summary["ci_upper"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_upper"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_upper"],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[1],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    resid_var["ci_upper"]
  )
)

# ================================
# 9. Posterior distributions of fixed effects
# ================================
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(
    b_baseline_Intercept,
    b_baseline_sexM,
    b_t0_Intercept,
    b_k_Intercept
  )

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>%
  select(-c(.chain, .iteration, .draw)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),
               fill = "skyblue",
               color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
    x = "Estimate",
    y = "Parameter"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 10. Access key derived quantities
# ================================
post$baseline_sexF
post$baseline_sexM
post$t0
post$rate_days_score_sexF
post$rate_days_score_sexM

# ================================
# 11. Posterior correlation between baseline and t0
# ================================
# Use the updated model with correlated random effect
draws_df <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# Check for correlation parameters
names(draws_df)[grepl("cor_rings_comb", names(draws_df))]
# Should return: "cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept"

# Extract posterior draws
cor_baseline_t0_post <- draws_df$cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept

# Posterior summary
cor_baseline_t0_summary <- c(
  median   = median(cor_baseline_t0_post),
  ci_lower = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.025),
  ci_upper = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.975)
)

cor_baseline_t0_summary

# Optional: visualize posterior correlation
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data.frame(cor = cor_baseline_t0_post), aes(x = cor)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(cor_baseline_t0_post), color = "darkblue", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Correlation between baseline and t0 (individual level)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Posterior distribution of correlation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 12. Posterior predictive checks
# ================================
library(brms)
# Plot observed vs simulated data
pp_check(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, ndraws = 100)

# Optional: numeric summaries for PPC
pp_draws <- posterior_predict(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, draws = 1000)
pp_summary <- apply(pp_draws, 2, function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sd = sd(x)))
head(pp_summary)

# ================================
# 13. R-hat checks for convergence
# ================================
rhat_values <- rhat(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)
summary(rhat_values)  # All should be ~1 for good convergence
```

### Sex-specific baseline, t0, and last_breeding_date_J_snum

```{r brms, eval = FALSE}
# Clean data
dat_sex_breeding <- dat_breeding %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>%
  # subset to columns of interest
  select(score, date_J, rings_comb_simp, sex, season, last_breeding_date_J_snum) %>%
  rename(rings_comb = rings_comb_simp) %>% 
  # reduce to distinct data
  distinct() %>% 
  # classify score and sex
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(score),
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
    rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb)
  ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  # drop NAs
  drop_na(score, date_J, sex, rings_comb, season, last_breeding_date_J_snum) %>% 
  # convert to dataframe
  as.data.frame()

# see what the mean and SD are for date_J
scaler <- scale(dat_sex_breeding$date_J)
attr(scaler, "scaled:center")  # mean
attr(scaler, "scaled:scale")   # SD

# Formula specification
brms_formula_cor_re_sex_baseline_sex_last_breed_t0 <- bf(
  score ~ 1 + (baseline - 1) * (1 - 1 / (1 + exp(-k * (date_J_scaled - t0)))),
  baseline ~ sex + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  t0 ~ sex + last_breeding_date_J_snum + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  # not enough temporal coverage within individuals over the moult to reliably 
  # estimate the 'quickness' of the moult, so better to simply assese the intercept
  k ~ 1,
  nl = TRUE
)

# Prior specification
priors <- c(
  # informative prior for the baseline:
  # centered around 5.5 with some uncertainty (SD = 1)
  # lb = 0 ensures that the baseline cannot be negative, which makes biological sense
  prior(normal(5.5, 1), nlpar = "baseline", lb = 0),
  
  # weakly informative (over ~1 month range: ~day 109 to ~165), helping 
  # convergence and avoiding unrealistic late- or early-season inflection points 
  # without being too restrictive 
  prior(normal(0, 0.5), nlpar = "t0"),
  
  # weakly informative prior reflecting a belief that the transition is not 
  # instantaneous (very high k) or flat (very low k), but somewhere in the middle.
  # The SD covers a reasonable range of transition steepness given the temporal scale.
  # lb = 0 ensures a positive slope, avoiding biologically implausible inverse dynamics 
  # but note that the non-linear function's formula has -k, so it will be forced to a
  # negative slope.
  prior(normal(1, 0.5), nlpar = "k", lb = 0),
  
  # These define group-level standard deviations (variability) for the baseline 
  # and t0 parameters across individuals (rings_comb) and individuals nested within 
  # season (rings_comb:season)
  # exponential(2) is a weakly regularizing prior that favors smaller variation 
  # but still allows for larger SDs if supported by the data.
  # The exponential distribution has a mode at 0 but a long right tail, which 
  # makes it suitable when you expect many individuals to vary only modestly, 
  # but allow for occasional larger deviations.
  # These are standard weakly informative priors for hierarchical models where 
  # you expect some among-individual or within-individual-over-season variation, 
  # but want to discourage extreme variance unless strongly supported.
  # Using the same prior across groups ensures comparability and avoids unintentional 
  # weighting of one grouping level.
  # They are compatible with the principle of regularization, which improves 
  # model stability and predictive performance.
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season")
)

# Fit model
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_sex_last_breed_t0 <- brm(
  formula = brms_formula_cor_re_sex_baseline_sex_last_breed_t0,
  data = dat_sex_breeding,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999, max_treedepth = 15),
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  iter = 10000,
  warmup = 5000,
  backend = "cmdstanr"
)

# write output to  disk
saveRDS(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_t0, file = here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_t0_2.rds"))
```

```{r}
# ============================
# Load required packages
# ============================
library(brms)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# ============================
# 1. Extract posterior samples
# ============================
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_sex_last_breed_t0)

# ================================
# 7. Variance components & repeatabilities
# ================================
post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    var_baseline_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2,
    var_t0_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2,
    R_baseline = var_baseline_indiv / (var_baseline_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_t0       = var_t0_indiv / (var_t0_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_baseline_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2),
    R_t0_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2)
  )

R_baseline_summary <- 
  summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline)
R_t0_summary                     <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0)
R_baseline_within_season_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline_within_season)
R_t0_within_season_summary       <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0_within_season)

R_summary <- 
  bind_rows(R_baseline_summary, R_t0_summary, R_baseline_within_season_summary, R_t0_within_season_summary) %>%
  mutate(Effect_CI = paste0(round(median, 3), " [", 
                            round(`ci_lower.2.5%`, 3), ", ", 
                            round(`ci_upper.97.5%`, 3), "]")) %>% 
  mutate(Parameter = c("R_baseline", "R_t0", "R_baseline_within_season", "R_t0_within_season"))

# ----------------------------
# 1a. Fixed effects
# ----------------------------
# Summarise all desired parameters
post_summary <- post %>%
  summarise(
    # Fixed effects
    median.b_baseline_Intercept           = median(b_baseline_Intercept),
    ci_lower.b_baseline_Intercept         = quantile(b_baseline_Intercept, 0.025),
    ci_upper.b_baseline_Intercept         = quantile(b_baseline_Intercept, 0.975),
    
    median.b_baseline_sexM                = median(b_baseline_sexM),
    ci_lower.b_baseline_sexM              = quantile(b_baseline_sexM, 0.025),
    ci_upper.b_baseline_sexM              = quantile(b_baseline_sexM, 0.975),
    
    median.b_t0_Intercept                 = median(b_t0_Intercept),
    ci_lower.b_t0_Intercept               = quantile(b_t0_Intercept, 0.025),
    ci_upper.b_t0_Intercept               = quantile(b_t0_Intercept, 0.975),
    
    median.b_t0_sexM                      = median(b_t0_sexM),
    ci_lower.b_t0_sexM                    = quantile(b_t0_sexM, 0.025),
    ci_upper.b_t0_sexM                    = quantile(b_t0_sexM, 0.975),
    
    median.b_t0_last_breeding_date        = median(b_t0_last_breeding_date_J_snum),
    ci_lower.b_t0_last_breeding_date      = quantile(b_t0_last_breeding_date_J_snum, 0.025),
    ci_upper.b_t0_last_breeding_date      = quantile(b_t0_last_breeding_date_J_snum, 0.975),
    
    median.b_k                            = median(b_k_Intercept),
    ci_lower.b_k                          = quantile(b_k_Intercept, 0.025),
    ci_upper.b_k                          = quantile(b_k_Intercept, 0.975),
    
    # Random effects SDs
    median.sd_ind_beta                     = median(sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept),
    ci_lower.sd_ind_beta                   = quantile(sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept, 0.025),
    ci_upper.sd_ind_beta                   = quantile(sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept, 0.975),
    
    median.sd_ind_t0                       = median(sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept),
    ci_lower.sd_ind_t0                     = quantile(sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept, 0.025),
    ci_upper.sd_ind_t0                     = quantile(sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept, 0.975),
    
    median.sd_indseason_beta               = median(`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`),
    ci_lower.sd_indseason_beta             = quantile(`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`, 0.025),
    ci_upper.sd_indseason_beta             = quantile(`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`, 0.975),
    
    median.sd_indseason_t0                 = median(`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`),
    ci_lower.sd_indseason_t0               = quantile(`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`, 0.025),
    ci_upper.sd_indseason_t0               = quantile(`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`, 0.975),
    
    # Random-effect correlation
    median.cor_beta_t0                     = median(cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept),
    ci_lower.cor_beta_t0                   = quantile(cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept, 0.025),
    ci_upper.cor_beta_t0                   = quantile(cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept, 0.975),
    
    # Residual SD
    median.sigma                           = median(sigma),
    ci_lower.sigma                         = quantile(sigma, 0.025),
    ci_upper.sigma                         = quantile(sigma, 0.975)
  ) %>%
  
  # Pivot to tidy format: Parameter | Median | CI_lower | CI_upper
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = c("Stat", "Parameter"),
               names_sep = "\\.",
               values_to = "Value") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Stat, values_from = Value) %>%
  
  # Optional: create formatted column
  mutate(Effect_CI = paste0(round(median, 3), " [", round(ci_lower, 3), ", ", round(ci_upper, 3), "]"))

post_summary

# ============================
# 2. Random effects: SDs and correlations
# ============================
vc <- VarCorr(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_sex_last_breed_t0)

# Individual-level SDs
sd_ind_beta <- vc$rings_comb$sd["baseline_Intercept", "Estimate"]
sd_ind_t0   <- vc$rings_comb$sd["t0_Intercept", "Estimate"]

# Individual-level correlation between baseline and t0
rho_beta_t0 <- vc$rings_comb$cor["baseline_Intercept", "Estimate", "t0_Intercept"]

# Individual-season SDs
sd_indseason_beta <- vc$`rings_comb:season`$sd["baseline_Intercept", "Estimate"]
sd_indseason_t0   <- vc$`rings_comb:season`$sd["t0_Intercept", "Estimate"]

# Residual SDs
resid_sd <- vc$residual$sd[1]

# ============================
# 3. Repeatabilities
# ============================
# Variances
var_ind_beta      <- sd_ind_beta^2
var_ind_t0        <- sd_ind_t0^2
var_indseason_beta <- sd_indseason_beta^2
var_indseason_t0   <- sd_indseason_t0^2
var_resid          <- resid_sd^2

# Individual-level repeatability
R_ind_beta <- var_ind_beta / (var_ind_beta + var_indseason_beta + var_resid)
R_ind_t0   <- var_ind_t0   / (var_ind_t0 + var_indseason_t0 + var_resid)

# Within-season repeatability
R_ws_beta <- var_indseason_beta / (var_ind_beta + var_indseason_beta + var_resid)
R_ws_t0   <- var_indseason_t0   / (var_ind_t0 + var_indseason_t0 + var_resid_t0)

# ============================
# 4. Marginal and conditional R²
# ============================
r2 <- bayes_R2(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_sex_last_breed_t0)
R2_marginal   <- mean(r2$R2_marginal)
R2_conditional <- mean(r2$R2_conditional)

r2_df <- r2_bayes(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_sex_last_breed_t0) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_summary <- tibble(
  type     = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
  mean     = c(r2_df$R2[2], r2_df$R2[1]),
  ci_lower = c(r2_df$CI_low[2], r2_df$CI_low[1]),
  ci_upper = c(r2_df$CI_high[2], r2_df$CI_high[1])
) %>% 
  mutate(Mean_CI = paste0(round(mean, 3), " [", round(ci_lower, 3), ", ", round(ci_upper, 3), "]"))

# ============================
# 5. Compile final table
# ============================
results_table <- tibble(
  Parameter = c(
    "Baseline intercept",
    "Sex (male vs female) - baseline",
    "t0 intercept",
    "Sex (male vs female) - t0",
    "Breeding phenology (last_date_breed) - t0",
    "Steepness k",
    "Individual baseline SD",
    "Individual t0 SD",
    "Individual-season baseline SD",
    "Individual-season t0 SD",
    "Random-effect correlation (baseline vs t0)",
    "Individual baseline repeatability",
    "Individual t0 repeatability",
    "Within-season baseline repeatability",
    "Within-season t0 repeatability",
    "Marginal R²",
    "Conditional R²"
  ),
  Estimate = c(
    post_summary$Effect_CI[1],
    post_summary$Effect_CI[2],
    post_summary$Effect_CI[3],
    post_summary$Effect_CI[4],
    post_summary$Effect_CI[5],
    post_summary$Effect_CI[6],
    post_summary$Effect_CI[7],
    post_summary$Effect_CI[8],
    post_summary$Effect_CI[9],
    post_summary$Effect_CI[10],
    post_summary$Effect_CI[11],
    R_summary$Effect_CI[1],
    R_summary$Effect_CI[2],
    R_summary$Effect_CI[3],
    R_summary$Effect_CI[4],
    r2_summary$Mean_CI[1],
    r2_summary$Mean_CI[2]
  )
)

# Print table
print(results_table)

```


```{r}
# ================================
# 1. Load model and inspect
# ================================
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_agec_baseline_agec_t0 <- readRDS(
  here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_agec_baseline_agec_t0_2.rds")
)

# Model summary and diagnostics
summary(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_last_breed_t0)
plot(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)
variables(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)

# ================================
# 2. Extract posterior draws
# ================================
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# ----------------
# Standardized sex effect on baseline
resid_sd <- sd(dat_out_rm$score, na.rm = TRUE)
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd

# ----------------
# Compute sex-specific baseline medians and 95% CI
baseline_sex_summary <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_F = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_M = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    median_F     = median(baseline_F),
    ci_lower_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.025),
    ci_upper_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.975),
    median_M     = median(baseline_M),
    ci_lower_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.025),
    ci_upper_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.975)
  )

baseline_sex_summary

# ================================
# 3. Back-transform t0 and baseline
# ================================
date_scaling <- dat_out_rm %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_J = mean(date_J),
    sd_J   = sd(date_J),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mean_date_sd   <- mean(date_scaling$sd_J)
mean_date_mean <- mean(date_scaling$mean_J)

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # sex-specific baseline
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,

    # t0 is NOT sex-specific in this model
    t0_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0        = t0_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean
  )

# ================================
# 4. Posterior summary helper
# ================================
summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    median   = median(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# ----------------
# Summarize key fixed effects
std_sex_baseline_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_baseline_std)
t0_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$t0)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
t0_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))

t0_summary_trans

# ================================
# 5. Interpret k: peak rate of change in days·score⁻¹
# ================================
date_sd <- mean_date_sd

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # Peak rate in scores·day⁻¹
    rate_scores_day_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_scores_day_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    # Convert to days·score⁻¹
    rate_days_score_sexF = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexF,
    rate_days_score_sexM = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexM
  )

rate_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexF)
rate_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexM)

# ================================
# 6. Bayesian R²
# ================================
r2_df <- r2_bayes(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_summary <- tibble(
  type     = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
  mean     = c(r2_df$R2[2], r2_df$R2[1]),
  ci_lower = c(r2_df$CI_low[2], r2_df$CI_low[1]),
  ci_upper = c(r2_df$CI_high[2], r2_df$CI_high[1])
)

# ================================
# 7. Variance components & repeatabilities
# ================================
post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    var_baseline_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2,
    var_t0_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2,
    R_baseline = var_baseline_indiv / (var_baseline_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_t0       = var_t0_indiv / (var_t0_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_baseline_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2),
    R_t0_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2)
  )

R_baseline_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline)
R_t0_summary                     <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0)
R_baseline_within_season_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline_within_season)
R_t0_within_season_summary       <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0_within_season)

# ================================
# 8. Variance component table
# ================================
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2
)

var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2
)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

tibble(
  parameter = c(
    "Std. effect size (sex) on baseline",
    "Inflection point (t0)",
    "Median baseline (F)",
    "Median baseline (M)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, F)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, M)",
    "Marginal R²",
    "Conditional R²",
    "Random effect variance (baseline)",
    "Random effect variance (t0)",
    "Residual variance"
  ),
  mean = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["median"],
    t0_summary["median"],
    baseline_sex_summary$median_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$median_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["median"],
    rate_sexM_summary["median"],
    r2_summary$mean[1],
    r2_summary$mean[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["median"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["median"],
    resid_var["median"]
  ),
  ci_lower = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_lower"],
    t0_summary["ci_lower"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_lower"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_lower"],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[1],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    resid_var["ci_lower"]
  ),
  ci_upper = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_upper"],
    t0_summary["ci_upper"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_upper"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_upper"],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[1],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    resid_var["ci_upper"]
  )
)

# ================================
# 9. Posterior distributions of fixed effects
# ================================
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(
    b_baseline_Intercept,
    b_baseline_sexM,
    b_t0_Intercept,
    b_k_Intercept
  )

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>%
  select(-c(.chain, .iteration, .draw)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),
               fill = "skyblue",
               color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
    x = "Estimate",
    y = "Parameter"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 10. Access key derived quantities
# ================================
post$baseline_sexF
post$baseline_sexM
post$t0
post$rate_days_score_sexF
post$rate_days_score_sexM

# ================================
# 11. Posterior correlation between baseline and t0
# ================================
# Use the updated model with correlated random effect
draws_df <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# Check for correlation parameters
names(draws_df)[grepl("cor_rings_comb", names(draws_df))]
# Should return: "cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept"

# Extract posterior draws
cor_baseline_t0_post <- draws_df$cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept

# Posterior summary
cor_baseline_t0_summary <- c(
  median   = median(cor_baseline_t0_post),
  ci_lower = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.025),
  ci_upper = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.975)
)

cor_baseline_t0_summary

# Optional: visualize posterior correlation
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data.frame(cor = cor_baseline_t0_post), aes(x = cor)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(cor_baseline_t0_post), color = "darkblue", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Correlation between baseline and t0 (individual level)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Posterior distribution of correlation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 12. Posterior predictive checks
# ================================
library(brms)
# Plot observed vs simulated data
pp_check(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, ndraws = 100)

# Optional: numeric summaries for PPC
pp_draws <- posterior_predict(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, draws = 1000)
pp_summary <- apply(pp_draws, 2, function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sd = sd(x)))
head(pp_summary)

# ================================
# 13. R-hat checks for convergence
# ================================
rhat_values <- rhat(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)
summary(rhat_values)  # All should be ~1 for good convergence
```


### Sex- and Age-class-specific baseline, t0, and last_breeding_date_J_snum

```{r brms, eval = FALSE}
# Clean data
dat_age_class <- dat_breeding %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>%
  # subset to columns of interest
  select(score, date_J, rings_comb_simp, sex, season, age, last_breeding_date_J_snum) %>%
  rename(rings_comb = rings_comb_simp) %>% 
  # reduce to distinct data
  distinct() %>% 
  # classify score and sex
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(score),
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
    rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb),
    age_class = factor(ifelse(age == 1, "FY", "AFY"), levels = c("FY", "AFY"))
  ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  # drop NAs
  drop_na(score, date_J, sex, age, age_class, rings_comb, season, last_breeding_date_J_snum) %>% 
  # convert to dataframe
  as.data.frame()

# see what the mean and SD are for date_J
scaler <- scale(dat_age_class$date_J)
attr(scaler, "scaled:center")  # mean
attr(scaler, "scaled:scale")   # SD

# Formula specification
brms_formula_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_last_breed_t0 <- bf(
  score ~ 1 + (baseline - 1) * (1 - 1 / (1 + exp(-k * (date_J_scaled - t0)))),
  baseline ~ sex + age_class + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  t0 ~ age_class + last_breeding_date_J_snum + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  # not enough temporal coverage within individuals over the moult to reliably 
  # estimate the 'quickness' of the moult, so better to simply assese the intercept
  k ~ 1,
  nl = TRUE
)

# Prior specification
priors <- c(
  # informative prior for the baseline:
  # centered around 5.5 with some uncertainty (SD = 1)
  # lb = 0 ensures that the baseline cannot be negative, which makes biological sense
  prior(normal(5.5, 1), nlpar = "baseline", lb = 0),
  
  # weakly informative (over ~1 month range: ~day 109 to ~165), helping 
  # convergence and avoiding unrealistic late- or early-season inflection points 
  # without being too restrictive 
  prior(normal(0, 0.5), nlpar = "t0"),
  
  # weakly informative prior reflecting a belief that the transition is not 
  # instantaneous (very high k) or flat (very low k), but somewhere in the middle.
  # The SD covers a reasonable range of transition steepness given the temporal scale.
  # lb = 0 ensures a positive slope, avoiding biologically implausible inverse dynamics 
  # but note that the non-linear function's formula has -k, so it will be forced to a
  # negative slope.
  prior(normal(1, 0.5), nlpar = "k", lb = 0),
  
  # These define group-level standard deviations (variability) for the baseline 
  # and t0 parameters across individuals (rings_comb) and individuals nested within 
  # season (rings_comb:season)
  # exponential(2) is a weakly regularizing prior that favors smaller variation 
  # but still allows for larger SDs if supported by the data.
  # The exponential distribution has a mode at 0 but a long right tail, which 
  # makes it suitable when you expect many individuals to vary only modestly, 
  # but allow for occasional larger deviations.
  # These are standard weakly informative priors for hierarchical models where 
  # you expect some among-individual or within-individual-over-season variation, 
  # but want to discourage extreme variance unless strongly supported.
  # Using the same prior across groups ensures comparability and avoids unintentional 
  # weighting of one grouping level.
  # They are compatible with the principle of regularization, which improves 
  # model stability and predictive performance.
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season")
)

# Fit model
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_last_breed_t0 <- brm(
  formula = brms_formula_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_last_breed_t0,
  data = dat_age_class,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999, max_treedepth = 15),
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  iter = 10000,
  warmup = 5000,
  backend = "cmdstanr"
)

# write output to  disk
saveRDS(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_t0, file = here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_t0_2.rds"))
```

```{r}
# ================================
# 1. Load model and inspect
# ================================
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_agec_baseline_agec_t0 <- readRDS(
  here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_agec_baseline_agec_t0_2.rds")
)

# Model summary and diagnostics
summary(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_last_breed_t0)
plot(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)
variables(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)

# ================================
# 2. Extract posterior draws
# ================================
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# ----------------
# Standardized sex effect on baseline
resid_sd <- sd(dat_out_rm$score, na.rm = TRUE)
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd

# ----------------
# Compute sex-specific baseline medians and 95% CI
baseline_sex_summary <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_F = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_M = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    median_F     = median(baseline_F),
    ci_lower_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.025),
    ci_upper_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.975),
    median_M     = median(baseline_M),
    ci_lower_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.025),
    ci_upper_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.975)
  )

baseline_sex_summary

# ================================
# 3. Back-transform t0 and baseline
# ================================
date_scaling <- dat_out_rm %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_J = mean(date_J),
    sd_J   = sd(date_J),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mean_date_sd   <- mean(date_scaling$sd_J)
mean_date_mean <- mean(date_scaling$mean_J)

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # sex-specific baseline
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,

    # t0 is NOT sex-specific in this model
    t0_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0        = t0_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean
  )

# ================================
# 4. Posterior summary helper
# ================================
summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    median   = median(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# ----------------
# Summarize key fixed effects
std_sex_baseline_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_baseline_std)
t0_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$t0)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
t0_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))

t0_summary_trans

# ================================
# 5. Interpret k: peak rate of change in days·score⁻¹
# ================================
date_sd <- mean_date_sd

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # Peak rate in scores·day⁻¹
    rate_scores_day_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_scores_day_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    # Convert to days·score⁻¹
    rate_days_score_sexF = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexF,
    rate_days_score_sexM = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexM
  )

rate_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexF)
rate_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexM)

# ================================
# 6. Bayesian R²
# ================================
r2_df <- r2_bayes(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_summary <- tibble(
  type     = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
  mean     = c(r2_df$R2[2], r2_df$R2[1]),
  ci_lower = c(r2_df$CI_low[2], r2_df$CI_low[1]),
  ci_upper = c(r2_df$CI_high[2], r2_df$CI_high[1])
)

# ================================
# 7. Variance components & repeatabilities
# ================================
post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    var_baseline_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2,
    var_t0_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2,
    R_baseline = var_baseline_indiv / (var_baseline_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_t0       = var_t0_indiv / (var_t0_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_baseline_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2),
    R_t0_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2)
  )

R_baseline_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline)
R_t0_summary                     <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0)
R_baseline_within_season_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline_within_season)
R_t0_within_season_summary       <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0_within_season)

# ================================
# 8. Variance component table
# ================================
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2
)

var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2
)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

tibble(
  parameter = c(
    "Std. effect size (sex) on baseline",
    "Inflection point (t0)",
    "Median baseline (F)",
    "Median baseline (M)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, F)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, M)",
    "Marginal R²",
    "Conditional R²",
    "Random effect variance (baseline)",
    "Random effect variance (t0)",
    "Residual variance"
  ),
  mean = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["median"],
    t0_summary["median"],
    baseline_sex_summary$median_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$median_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["median"],
    rate_sexM_summary["median"],
    r2_summary$mean[1],
    r2_summary$mean[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["median"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["median"],
    resid_var["median"]
  ),
  ci_lower = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_lower"],
    t0_summary["ci_lower"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_lower"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_lower"],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[1],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    resid_var["ci_lower"]
  ),
  ci_upper = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_upper"],
    t0_summary["ci_upper"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_upper"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_upper"],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[1],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    resid_var["ci_upper"]
  )
)

# ================================
# 9. Posterior distributions of fixed effects
# ================================
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(
    b_baseline_Intercept,
    b_baseline_sexM,
    b_t0_Intercept,
    b_k_Intercept
  )

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>%
  select(-c(.chain, .iteration, .draw)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),
               fill = "skyblue",
               color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
    x = "Estimate",
    y = "Parameter"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 10. Access key derived quantities
# ================================
post$baseline_sexF
post$baseline_sexM
post$t0
post$rate_days_score_sexF
post$rate_days_score_sexM

# ================================
# 11. Posterior correlation between baseline and t0
# ================================
# Use the updated model with correlated random effect
draws_df <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# Check for correlation parameters
names(draws_df)[grepl("cor_rings_comb", names(draws_df))]
# Should return: "cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept"

# Extract posterior draws
cor_baseline_t0_post <- draws_df$cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept

# Posterior summary
cor_baseline_t0_summary <- c(
  median   = median(cor_baseline_t0_post),
  ci_lower = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.025),
  ci_upper = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.975)
)

cor_baseline_t0_summary

# Optional: visualize posterior correlation
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data.frame(cor = cor_baseline_t0_post), aes(x = cor)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(cor_baseline_t0_post), color = "darkblue", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Correlation between baseline and t0 (individual level)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Posterior distribution of correlation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 12. Posterior predictive checks
# ================================
library(brms)
# Plot observed vs simulated data
pp_check(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, ndraws = 100)

# Optional: numeric summaries for PPC
pp_draws <- posterior_predict(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, draws = 1000)
pp_summary <- apply(pp_draws, 2, function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sd = sd(x)))
head(pp_summary)

# ================================
# 13. R-hat checks for convergence
# ================================
rhat_values <- rhat(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)
summary(rhat_values)  # All should be ~1 for good convergence
```

### Sex- and Age-class-specific baseline, t0, and first_breeding_date_J_snum

```{r brms, eval = FALSE}
# Clean data
dat_age_class <- dat_breeding %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>%
  # subset to columns of interest
  select(score, date_J, rings_comb_simp, sex, season, age, first_breeding_date_J_snum) %>%
  rename(rings_comb = rings_comb_simp) %>% 
  # reduce to distinct data
  distinct() %>% 
  # classify score and sex
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(score),
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
    rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb),
    age_class = factor(ifelse(age == 1, "FY", "AFY"), levels = c("FY", "AFY"))
  ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  # drop NAs
  drop_na(score, date_J, sex, age, age_class, rings_comb, season, first_breeding_date_J_snum) %>% 
  # convert to dataframe
  as.data.frame()

# see what the mean and SD are for date_J
scaler <- scale(dat_age_class$date_J)
attr(scaler, "scaled:center")  # mean
attr(scaler, "scaled:scale")   # SD

# Formula specification
brms_formula_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_first_breed_t0 <- bf(
  score ~ 1 + (baseline - 1) * (1 - 1 / (1 + exp(-k * (date_J_scaled - t0)))),
  baseline ~ sex + age_class + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  t0 ~ age_class + first_breeding_date_J_snum + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  # not enough temporal coverage within individuals over the moult to reliably 
  # estimate the 'quickness' of the moult, so better to simply assese the intercept
  k ~ 1,
  nl = TRUE
)

# Prior specification
priors <- c(
  # informative prior for the baseline:
  # centered around 5.5 with some uncertainty (SD = 1)
  # lb = 0 ensures that the baseline cannot be negative, which makes biological sense
  prior(normal(5.5, 1), nlpar = "baseline", lb = 0),
  
  # weakly informative (over ~1 month range: ~day 109 to ~165), helping 
  # convergence and avoiding unrealistic late- or early-season inflection points 
  # without being too restrictive 
  prior(normal(0, 0.5), nlpar = "t0"),
  
  # weakly informative prior reflecting a belief that the transition is not 
  # instantaneous (very high k) or flat (very low k), but somewhere in the middle.
  # The SD covers a reasonable range of transition steepness given the temporal scale.
  # lb = 0 ensures a positive slope, avoiding biologically implausible inverse dynamics 
  # but note that the non-linear function's formula has -k, so it will be forced to a
  # negative slope.
  prior(normal(1, 0.5), nlpar = "k", lb = 0),
  
  # These define group-level standard deviations (variability) for the baseline 
  # and t0 parameters across individuals (rings_comb) and individuals nested within 
  # season (rings_comb:season)
  # exponential(2) is a weakly regularizing prior that favors smaller variation 
  # but still allows for larger SDs if supported by the data.
  # The exponential distribution has a mode at 0 but a long right tail, which 
  # makes it suitable when you expect many individuals to vary only modestly, 
  # but allow for occasional larger deviations.
  # These are standard weakly informative priors for hierarchical models where 
  # you expect some among-individual or within-individual-over-season variation, 
  # but want to discourage extreme variance unless strongly supported.
  # Using the same prior across groups ensures comparability and avoids unintentional 
  # weighting of one grouping level.
  # They are compatible with the principle of regularization, which improves 
  # model stability and predictive performance.
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season")
)

# Fit model
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_first_breed_t0 <- brm(
  formula = brms_formula_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_first_breed_t0,
  data = dat_age_class,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999, max_treedepth = 15),
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  iter = 10000,
  warmup = 5000,
  backend = "cmdstanr"
)

# write output to  disk
saveRDS(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_t0, file = here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_t0_2.rds"))
```

```{r}
# ================================
# 1. Load model and inspect
# ================================
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_agec_baseline_agec_t0 <- readRDS(
  here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_agec_baseline_agec_t0_2.rds")
)

# Model summary and diagnostics
summary(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_first_breed_t0)
plot(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)
variables(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)

# ================================
# 2. Extract posterior draws
# ================================
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# ----------------
# Standardized sex effect on baseline
resid_sd <- sd(dat_out_rm$score, na.rm = TRUE)
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd

# ----------------
# Compute sex-specific baseline medians and 95% CI
baseline_sex_summary <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_F = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_M = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    median_F     = median(baseline_F),
    ci_lower_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.025),
    ci_upper_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.975),
    median_M     = median(baseline_M),
    ci_lower_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.025),
    ci_upper_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.975)
  )

baseline_sex_summary

# ================================
# 3. Back-transform t0 and baseline
# ================================
date_scaling <- dat_out_rm %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_J = mean(date_J),
    sd_J   = sd(date_J),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mean_date_sd   <- mean(date_scaling$sd_J)
mean_date_mean <- mean(date_scaling$mean_J)

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # sex-specific baseline
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,

    # t0 is NOT sex-specific in this model
    t0_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0        = t0_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean
  )

# ================================
# 4. Posterior summary helper
# ================================
summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    median   = median(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# ----------------
# Summarize key fixed effects
std_sex_baseline_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_baseline_std)
t0_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$t0)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
t0_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))

t0_summary_trans

# ================================
# 5. Interpret k: peak rate of change in days·score⁻¹
# ================================
date_sd <- mean_date_sd

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # Peak rate in scores·day⁻¹
    rate_scores_day_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_scores_day_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    # Convert to days·score⁻¹
    rate_days_score_sexF = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexF,
    rate_days_score_sexM = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexM
  )

rate_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexF)
rate_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexM)

# ================================
# 6. Bayesian R²
# ================================
r2_df <- r2_bayes(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_summary <- tibble(
  type     = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
  mean     = c(r2_df$R2[2], r2_df$R2[1]),
  ci_lower = c(r2_df$CI_low[2], r2_df$CI_low[1]),
  ci_upper = c(r2_df$CI_high[2], r2_df$CI_high[1])
)

# ================================
# 7. Variance components & repeatabilities
# ================================
post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    var_baseline_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2,
    var_t0_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2,
    R_baseline = var_baseline_indiv / (var_baseline_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_t0       = var_t0_indiv / (var_t0_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_baseline_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2),
    R_t0_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2)
  )

R_baseline_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline)
R_t0_summary                     <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0)
R_baseline_within_season_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline_within_season)
R_t0_within_season_summary       <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0_within_season)

# ================================
# 8. Variance component table
# ================================
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2
)

var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2
)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

tibble(
  parameter = c(
    "Std. effect size (sex) on baseline",
    "Inflection point (t0)",
    "Median baseline (F)",
    "Median baseline (M)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, F)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, M)",
    "Marginal R²",
    "Conditional R²",
    "Random effect variance (baseline)",
    "Random effect variance (t0)",
    "Residual variance"
  ),
  mean = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["median"],
    t0_summary["median"],
    baseline_sex_summary$median_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$median_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["median"],
    rate_sexM_summary["median"],
    r2_summary$mean[1],
    r2_summary$mean[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["median"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["median"],
    resid_var["median"]
  ),
  ci_lower = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_lower"],
    t0_summary["ci_lower"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_lower"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_lower"],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[1],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    resid_var["ci_lower"]
  ),
  ci_upper = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_upper"],
    t0_summary["ci_upper"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_upper"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_upper"],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[1],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    resid_var["ci_upper"]
  )
)

# ================================
# 9. Posterior distributions of fixed effects
# ================================
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(
    b_baseline_Intercept,
    b_baseline_sexM,
    b_t0_Intercept,
    b_k_Intercept
  )

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>%
  select(-c(.chain, .iteration, .draw)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),
               fill = "skyblue",
               color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
    x = "Estimate",
    y = "Parameter"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 10. Access key derived quantities
# ================================
post$baseline_sexF
post$baseline_sexM
post$t0
post$rate_days_score_sexF
post$rate_days_score_sexM

# ================================
# 11. Posterior correlation between baseline and t0
# ================================
# Use the updated model with correlated random effect
draws_df <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# Check for correlation parameters
names(draws_df)[grepl("cor_rings_comb", names(draws_df))]
# Should return: "cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept"

# Extract posterior draws
cor_baseline_t0_post <- draws_df$cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept

# Posterior summary
cor_baseline_t0_summary <- c(
  median   = median(cor_baseline_t0_post),
  ci_lower = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.025),
  ci_upper = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.975)
)

cor_baseline_t0_summary

# Optional: visualize posterior correlation
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data.frame(cor = cor_baseline_t0_post), aes(x = cor)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(cor_baseline_t0_post), color = "darkblue", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Correlation between baseline and t0 (individual level)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Posterior distribution of correlation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 12. Posterior predictive checks
# ================================
library(brms)
# Plot observed vs simulated data
pp_check(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, ndraws = 100)

# Optional: numeric summaries for PPC
pp_draws <- posterior_predict(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, draws = 1000)
pp_summary <- apply(pp_draws, 2, function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sd = sd(x)))
head(pp_summary)

# ================================
# 13. R-hat checks for convergence
# ================================
rhat_values <- rhat(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)
summary(rhat_values)  # All should be ~1 for good convergence
```
