### Sex- and Age-class-specific baseline, t0, and last_nest_first_obs_J_snum

```{r brms, eval = FALSE}
# Clean data
dat_age_class <- dat_breeding %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>%
  # subset to columns of interest
  select(score, date_J, rings_comb_simp, sex, season, age, last_nest_first_obs_J_snum) %>%
  rename(rings_comb = rings_comb_simp) %>% 
  # reduce to distinct data
  distinct() %>% 
  # classify score and sex
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(score),
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
    rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb),
    age_class = factor(ifelse(age == 1, "FY", "AFY"), levels = c("FY", "AFY"))
  ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  # drop NAs
  drop_na(score, date_J, sex, age, age_class, rings_comb, season, last_nest_first_obs_J_snum) %>% 
  # convert to dataframe
  as.data.frame()

# see what the mean and SD are for date_J
scaler <- scale(dat_age_class$date_J)
attr(scaler, "scaled:center")  # mean
attr(scaler, "scaled:scale")   # SD

# Formula specification
brms_formula_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_last_first_breed_t0 <- bf(
  score ~ 1 + (baseline - 1) * (1 - 1 / (1 + exp(-k * (date_J_scaled - t0)))),
  baseline ~ sex + age_class + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  t0 ~ age_class + last_nest_first_obs_J_snum + (1 | i | rings_comb) + (1 | rings_comb:season),
  # not enough temporal coverage within individuals over the moult to reliably 
  # estimate the 'quickness' of the moult, so better to simply assese the intercept
  k ~ 1,
  nl = TRUE
)

# Prior specification
priors <- c(
  # informative prior for the baseline:
  # centered around 5.5 with some uncertainty (SD = 1)
  # lb = 0 ensures that the baseline cannot be negative, which makes biological sense
  prior(normal(5.5, 1), nlpar = "baseline", lb = 0),
  
  # weakly informative (over ~1 month range: ~day 109 to ~165), helping 
  # convergence and avoiding unrealistic late- or early-season inflection points 
  # without being too restrictive 
  prior(normal(0, 0.5), nlpar = "t0"),
  
  # weakly informative prior reflecting a belief that the transition is not 
  # instantaneous (very high k) or flat (very low k), but somewhere in the middle.
  # The SD covers a reasonable range of transition steepness given the temporal scale.
  # lb = 0 ensures a positive slope, avoiding biologically implausible inverse dynamics 
  # but note that the non-linear function's formula has -k, so it will be forced to a
  # negative slope.
  prior(normal(1, 0.5), nlpar = "k", lb = 0),
  
  # These define group-level standard deviations (variability) for the baseline 
  # and t0 parameters across individuals (rings_comb) and individuals nested within 
  # season (rings_comb:season)
  # exponential(2) is a weakly regularizing prior that favors smaller variation 
  # but still allows for larger SDs if supported by the data.
  # The exponential distribution has a mode at 0 but a long right tail, which 
  # makes it suitable when you expect many individuals to vary only modestly, 
  # but allow for occasional larger deviations.
  # These are standard weakly informative priors for hierarchical models where 
  # you expect some among-individual or within-individual-over-season variation, 
  # but want to discourage extreme variance unless strongly supported.
  # Using the same prior across groups ensures comparability and avoids unintentional 
  # weighting of one grouping level.
  # They are compatible with the principle of regularization, which improves 
  # model stability and predictive performance.
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "baseline", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb"),
  prior(exponential(2), nlpar = "t0", class = "sd", group = "rings_comb:season")
)

# Fit model
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_last_first_breed_t0 <- brm(
  formula = brms_formula_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_last_first_breed_t0,
  data = dat_age_class,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999, max_treedepth = 15),
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  iter = 10000,
  warmup = 5000,
  backend = "cmdstanr"
)

# write output to  disk
saveRDS(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_t0, file = here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_t0_2.rds"))
```

```{r}
# ================================
# 1. Load model and inspect
# ================================
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_agec_baseline_agec_t0 <- readRDS(
  here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_agec_baseline_agec_t0_2.rds")
)

# Model summary and diagnostics
summary(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_age_class_baseline_age_class_last_first_breed_t0)
plot(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)
variables(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)

# ================================
# 2. Extract posterior draws
# ================================
post <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# ----------------
# Standardized sex effect on baseline
resid_sd <- sd(dat_out_rm$score, na.rm = TRUE)
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd

# ----------------
# Compute sex-specific baseline medians and 95% CI
baseline_sex_summary <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_F = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_M = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    median_F     = median(baseline_F),
    ci_lower_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.025),
    ci_upper_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.975),
    median_M     = median(baseline_M),
    ci_lower_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.025),
    ci_upper_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.975)
  )

baseline_sex_summary

# ================================
# 3. Back-transform t0 and baseline
# ================================
date_scaling <- dat_out_rm %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_J = mean(date_J),
    sd_J   = sd(date_J),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mean_date_sd   <- mean(date_scaling$sd_J)
mean_date_mean <- mean(date_scaling$mean_J)

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # sex-specific baseline
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,

    # t0 is NOT sex-specific in this model
    t0_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0        = t0_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean
  )

# ================================
# 4. Posterior summary helper
# ================================
summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    median   = median(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# ----------------
# Summarize key fixed effects
std_sex_baseline_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_baseline_std)
t0_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$t0)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
t0_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01"))

t0_summary_trans

# ================================
# 5. Interpret k: peak rate of change in days·score⁻¹
# ================================
date_sd <- mean_date_sd

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # Peak rate in scores·day⁻¹
    rate_scores_day_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_scores_day_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    # Convert to days·score⁻¹
    rate_days_score_sexF = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexF,
    rate_days_score_sexM = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexM
  )

rate_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexF)
rate_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexM)

# ================================
# 6. Bayesian R²
# ================================
r2_df <- r2_bayes(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_summary <- tibble(
  type     = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
  mean     = c(r2_df$R2[2], r2_df$R2[1]),
  ci_lower = c(r2_df$CI_low[2], r2_df$CI_low[1]),
  ci_upper = c(r2_df$CI_high[2], r2_df$CI_high[1])
)

# ================================
# 7. Variance components & repeatabilities
# ================================
post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    var_baseline_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2,
    var_t0_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2,
    R_baseline = var_baseline_indiv / (var_baseline_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_t0       = var_t0_indiv / (var_t0_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_baseline_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2),
    R_t0_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2)
  )

R_baseline_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline)
R_t0_summary                     <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0)
R_baseline_within_season_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline_within_season)
R_t0_within_season_summary       <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0_within_season)

# ================================
# 8. Variance component table
# ================================
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2
)

var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2
)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

tibble(
  parameter = c(
    "Std. effect size (sex) on baseline",
    "Inflection point (t0)",
    "Median baseline (F)",
    "Median baseline (M)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, F)",
    "Peak rate (days·score⁻¹, M)",
    "Marginal R²",
    "Conditional R²",
    "Random effect variance (baseline)",
    "Random effect variance (t0)",
    "Residual variance"
  ),
  mean = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["median"],
    t0_summary["median"],
    baseline_sex_summary$median_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$median_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["median"],
    rate_sexM_summary["median"],
    r2_summary$mean[1],
    r2_summary$mean[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["median"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["median"],
    resid_var["median"]
  ),
  ci_lower = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_lower"],
    t0_summary["ci_lower"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_lower"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_lower"],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[1],
    r2_summary$ci_lower[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower"],
    resid_var["ci_lower"]
  ),
  ci_upper = c(
    std_sex_baseline_summary["ci_upper"],
    t0_summary["ci_upper"],
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_F,
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_M,
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_upper"],
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_upper"],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[1],
    r2_summary$ci_upper[2],
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper"],
    resid_var["ci_upper"]
  )
)

# ================================
# 9. Posterior distributions of fixed effects
# ================================
posterior_fixed <- fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(
    b_baseline_Intercept,
    b_baseline_sexM,
    b_t0_Intercept,
    b_k_Intercept
  )

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>%
  select(-c(.chain, .iteration, .draw)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),
               fill = "skyblue",
               color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
    x = "Estimate",
    y = "Parameter"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 10. Access key derived quantities
# ================================
post$baseline_sexF
post$baseline_sexM
post$t0
post$rate_days_score_sexF
post$rate_days_score_sexM

# ================================
# 11. Posterior correlation between baseline and t0
# ================================
# Use the updated model with correlated random effect
draws_df <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)

# Check for correlation parameters
names(draws_df)[grepl("cor_rings_comb", names(draws_df))]
# Should return: "cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept"

# Extract posterior draws
cor_baseline_t0_post <- draws_df$cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept

# Posterior summary
cor_baseline_t0_summary <- c(
  median   = median(cor_baseline_t0_post),
  ci_lower = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.025),
  ci_upper = quantile(cor_baseline_t0_post, 0.975)
)

cor_baseline_t0_summary

# Optional: visualize posterior correlation
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data.frame(cor = cor_baseline_t0_post), aes(x = cor)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(cor_baseline_t0_post), color = "darkblue", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Correlation between baseline and t0 (individual level)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Posterior distribution of correlation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ================================
# 12. Posterior predictive checks
# ================================
library(brms)
# Plot observed vs simulated data
pp_check(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, ndraws = 100)

# Optional: numeric summaries for PPC
pp_draws <- posterior_predict(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2, draws = 1000)
pp_summary <- apply(pp_draws, 2, function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sd = sd(x)))
head(pp_summary)

# ================================
# 13. R-hat checks for convergence
# ================================
rhat_values <- rhat(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_2)
summary(rhat_values)  # All should be ~1 for good convergence
```



### Bayesian non-linear overall effects - sex, age, age_class, and migratory status

#### posterior distributions of baseline and t0

```{r}
# bring the output into the R environment
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0_.rds"))
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0_.rds"))
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0.rds"))
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_.rds"))

# back-transform the t0 effect size
# Extract scaling parameters from your original scaling
dat_out_rm <- 
  dat %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300)

date_J_mean <- attr(scale(dat_out_rm$date_J), "scaled:center")
date_J_sd   <- attr(scale(dat_out_rm$date_J), "scaled:scale")

# Extract only fixed effects
posterior_fixed_dot <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(b_baseline_Intercept, b_baseline_sexM,
               b_t0_Intercept,
               b_k_Intercept) %>% 
  select(-c(`.chain`, `.iteration`, `.draw`)) %>%
  mutate(
    t0_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM) %>% 
  mutate(
    t0 = t0_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate") %>% 
  mutate(model = "dot")

posterior_fixed_sex <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(b_baseline_Intercept, b_baseline_sexM,
               b_t0_Intercept, b_t0_sexM,
               b_k_Intercept) %>% 
  select(-c(`.chain`, `.iteration`, `.draw`)) %>%
  mutate(
    t0_sexF_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_sexM_scaled = b_t0_Intercept + b_t0_sexM,
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM) %>% 
  mutate(
    t0_sexF = t0_sexF_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean,
    t0_sexM = t0_sexM_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean
  ) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate") %>% 
  mutate(model = "sex")

posterior_fixed_age_class <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(b_baseline_Intercept, b_baseline_sexM,
               b_t0_Intercept, b_t0_age_classAFY,
               b_k_Intercept) %>% 
  select(-c(`.chain`, `.iteration`, `.draw`)) %>%
  mutate(
    t0_age_classFY_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_age_classAFY_scaled = b_t0_Intercept + b_t0_age_classAFY,
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM) %>% 
  mutate(
    t0_age_classFY = t0_age_classFY_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean,
    t0_age_classAFY = t0_age_classAFY_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean
  ) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate") %>% 
  mutate(model = "age class")

posterior_fixed_status <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0 %>%
  spread_draws(b_baseline_Intercept, b_baseline_sexM,
               b_t0_Intercept, b_t0_migratory_statusR,
               b_k_Intercept) %>% 
  select(-c(`.chain`, `.iteration`, `.draw`)) %>%
  mutate(
    t0_migratory_statusM_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_migratory_statusR_scaled = b_t0_Intercept + b_t0_migratory_statusR,
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM) %>% 
  mutate(
    t0_migratory_statusM = t0_migratory_statusM_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean,
    t0_migratory_statusR = t0_migratory_statusR_scaled * date_J_sd + date_J_mean
  ) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate") %>% 
  mutate(model = "migratory status")

posterior_long <- 
  bind_rows(posterior_fixed_dot, posterior_fixed_sex, posterior_fixed_age_class, posterior_fixed_status)

july_origin <- as.Date("2024-07-01")  # adjust year as needed

jday_to_date <- function(jday) {
  format(july_origin + jday - 1, "%d %b")
}

dot_t0_post_plot <- 
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", "t0",
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "dot"), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), slab_fill = "grey50", 
               color = "black", point_fill = "white", shape = 23, slab_alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(x = "Inflection point estimate of pre-basic moult", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.1),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(10, 5.5, 10, 5.5), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "t0_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "t0_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "t0_sexF" = "Female",
  "t0_sexM" = "Male",
  "t0" = "All birds",
  "t0_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "t0_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model",
  "dot" = "Intercept only model"
))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(150, 260),
    breaks = seq(150, 260, by = 21),  # or choose your preferred ticks
    labels = jday_to_date)

sex_t0_post_plot <- 
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "sex"), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term, fill = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), color = "black", point_fill = "white", shape = 23, slab_alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(x = "Inflection point estimate of pre-basic moult", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.1),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(-10, 5.5, -5, 10), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "t0_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "t0_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "t0_sexF" = "Female",
  "t0_sexM" = "Male",
  "t0_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "t0_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(150, 260),
    breaks = seq(150, 260, by = 21),  # or choose your preferred ticks
    labels = jday_to_date) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))

status_t0_post_plot <- 
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "migratory status")%>%
    mutate(term = factor(term, levels = c(
      "t0_migratory_statusR",  # Resident

    ))), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term, fill = term)) +

  labs(x = "Date of pre-basic moult\ninflection point", y = "Parameter") +

        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 15), colour = "grey40", size = 11),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.1),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(-10, 5.5, -20, 4), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "t0_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "t0_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "t0_sexF" = "Female",
  "t0_sexM" = "Male",
  "t0_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "t0_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
  scale_x_continuous(

    breaks = seq(150, 260, by = 21),  # or choose your preferred ticks
    labels = jday_to_date) +

age_class_t0_post_plot <- 
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "age class"),

        axis.title.y = element_blank(),

        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),

  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "t0_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "t0_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "t0_sexF" = "Female",
  "t0_sexM" = "Male",
  "t0_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "t0_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(150, 260),
    breaks = seq(150, 260, by = 21),  # or choose your preferred ticks
    labels = jday_to_date) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#59A14F", "#E15759"))

dot_baseline_post_plot <- 
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("baseline_migratory_statusM", "baseline_migratory_statusR", 
                            "baseline_sexF", "baseline_sexM",
                            "baseline_age_classFY", "baseline_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "dot"), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term, fill = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), color = "black", point_fill = "white", shape = 21, slab_alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(x = "Rufous band score prior to pre-basic moult", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.1),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(-10, 5.5, -5, 10), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "baseline_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "baseline_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "baseline_sexF" = "Female",
  "baseline_sexM" = "Male",
  "baseline_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "baseline_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(4, 7),
    breaks = seq(4, 7, by = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))

sex_baseline_post_plot <- 
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("baseline_migratory_statusM", "baseline_migratory_statusR", 
                            "baseline_sexF", "baseline_sexM", 
                            "baseline_age_classFY", "baseline_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "sex"), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term, fill = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), color = "black", point_fill = "white", shape = 21, slab_alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(x = "Rufous band score prior to pre-basic moult", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.1),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(-10, 5.5, -5, 10), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "baseline_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "baseline_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "baseline_sexF" = "Female",
  "baseline_sexM" = "Male",
  "baseline_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "baseline_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(4, 7),
    breaks = seq(4, 7, by = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))

status_baseline_post_plot <- 
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("baseline_migratory_statusM", "baseline_migratory_statusR", 
                            "baseline_sexF", "baseline_sexM", 
                            "baseline_age_classFY", "baseline_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "migratory status"), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term, fill = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0, 0.8, 0.95), color = "black", point_fill = "white", shape = 21, slab_alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(x = "Rufous band score\nprior to pre-basic moult", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 38), colour = "grey40", size = 11),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.1),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(-10, 5.5, -20, 10), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "baseline_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "baseline_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "baseline_sexF" = "Female",
  "baseline_sexM" = "Male",
  "baseline_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "baseline_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(4, 7),
    breaks = seq(4, 7, by = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))

age_class_baseline_post_plot <- 
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("baseline_migratory_statusM", "baseline_migratory_statusR", 
                            "baseline_sexF", "baseline_sexM", 
                            "baseline_age_classFY", "baseline_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "age class"), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term, fill = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), color = "black", point_fill = "white", shape = 21, slab_alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(x = "Rufous band score prior to pre-basic moult", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.1),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(-10, 5.5, -5, 10), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "baseline_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "baseline_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "baseline_sexF" = "Female",
  "baseline_sexM" = "Male",
  "baseline_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "baseline_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(4, 7),
    breaks = seq(4, 7, by = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))

dot_model_plot <- 
  wrap_elements(dot_t0_post_plot) + wrap_elements(dot_baseline_post_plot) #+
  # plot_annotation(
  #   title = "Null model (no state-dependent timing)",
  #   theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, face = "italic"),
  #                 text = element_text(family = "Verdana"))
  # )

sex_model_plot <- 
  wrap_elements(sex_t0_post_plot) + wrap_elements(sex_baseline_post_plot) #+
  # plot_annotation(
  #   title = "Sex model",
  #   theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, face = "italic"),
  #                 text = element_text(family = "Verdana"))
  # )

age_class_model_plot <- 
  wrap_elements(age_class_t0_post_plot) + wrap_elements(age_class_baseline_post_plot) #+
  # plot_annotation(
  #   title = "Age class model",
  #   theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, face = "italic"),
  #                 text = element_text(family = "Verdana"))
  # )

status_model_plot <- 
  wrap_elements(status_t0_post_plot) + wrap_elements(status_baseline_post_plot) #+
  # plot_annotation(
  #   title = "Migratory strategy model",
  #   theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, face = "italic"),
  #                 text = element_text(family = "Verdana"))
  # )

final_plot <- 
  (wrap_elements(dot_model_plot) +
   wrap_elements(sex_model_plot) + 
   wrap_elements(age_class_model_plot) + 
   wrap_elements(status_model_plot)) +
  plot_layout(ncol = 1, #guides = "keep",
    heights = c(1, 1, 1, 1.5)) & theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 5.5, 0))

final_plot
```

### population-level curves

```{r}
# bring the output into the R environment
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0_.rds"))
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0_.rds"))
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0.rds"))
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/Jan_2026/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0_.rds"))

dat_ <- 
  dat %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>% 
  # # classify score and sex
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>%
  # ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J)),
         date_J_sd = sd(date_J),
         date_J_mean = mean(date_J))

dates_for_plot <- 
  data.frame(date_ = as.Date(c(as.Date("2021-07-01"):as.Date("2022-06-30"))), 
             date_J = c(1:365))

# Create new data for prediction
newdata_sex_pop_prediction <- 
  expand.grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  sex = c("F", "M"),
  rings_comb = NA,   # exclude group-level effects
  season = NA
) %>% 
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

newdata_age_class_pop_prediction <- 
  expand.grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  sex = c("F", "M"),
  age_class = c("FY", "AFY"),
  rings_comb = NA,   # exclude group-level effects
  season = NA
) %>% 
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

newdata_status_pop_prediction <- 
  expand.grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  sex = c("F", "M"),
  migratory_status = c("R", "M"),
  rings_comb = NA,   # exclude group-level effects
  season = NA
) %>% 
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

newdata_dot_pop_prediction <- 
  expand.grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  sex = c("F", "M"),
  rings_comb = NA,   # exclude group-level effects
  season = NA
) %>% 
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

fitted_vals_sex <- fitted(
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0,
  newdata = newdata_sex_pop_prediction,
  re_formula = NA,  # exclude random effects
  summary = TRUE
)

fitted_vals_all <- fitted(
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0,
  newdata = newdata_dot_pop_prediction,
  re_formula = NA,  # exclude random effects
  summary = TRUE
)

fitted_vals_age_class <- fitted(
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0,
  newdata = newdata_age_class_pop_prediction,
  re_formula = NA,  # exclude random effects
  summary = TRUE
)

fitted_vals_status <- fitted(
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0,
  newdata = newdata_status_pop_prediction,
  re_formula = NA,  # exclude random effects
  summary = TRUE
)

newdata_sex_pop_prediction$fit <- fitted_vals_sex[, "Estimate"]
newdata_sex_pop_prediction$lower <- fitted_vals_sex[, "Q2.5"]
newdata_sex_pop_prediction$upper <- fitted_vals_sex[, "Q97.5"]

newdata_dot_pop_prediction$fit <- fitted_vals_all[, "Estimate"]
newdata_dot_pop_prediction$lower <- fitted_vals_all[, "Q2.5"]
newdata_dot_pop_prediction$upper <- fitted_vals_all[, "Q97.5"]

newdata_age_class_pop_prediction$fit <- fitted_vals_age_class[, "Estimate"]
newdata_age_class_pop_prediction$lower <- fitted_vals_age_class[, "Q2.5"]
newdata_age_class_pop_prediction$upper <- fitted_vals_age_class[, "Q97.5"]

newdata_status_pop_prediction$fit <- fitted_vals_status[, "Estimate"]
newdata_status_pop_prediction$lower <- fitted_vals_status[, "Q2.5"]
newdata_status_pop_prediction$upper <- fitted_vals_status[, "Q97.5"]

# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0$data %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex)

# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex_dot <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0$data %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex)

# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex_status <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0$data %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex, migratory_status)

# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex_age_class <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0$data %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex, age_class)

# Expand grid for predictions
newdata_age_class <- expand_grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  id_season_sex_age_class
)

newdata_sex <- expand_grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  id_season_sex
)

newdata_status <- expand_grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  id_season_sex_status
)


newdata_sex_dot <- expand_grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  id_season_sex
)

# Get predicted curves with uncertainty
newdata_sex_ind_prediction <- 
  add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0, 
                  newdata = newdata_sex, 
                  ndraws = 50) %>%   # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

newdata_sex_ind_prediction_RRGG_RGGL <- 
  add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0, 
                  newdata = newdata_sex, 
                  ndraws = 50) %>%   # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J") %>% 
  filter(rings_comb %in% c("RRGG", "RGGL"))

newdata_status_ind_prediction <- 
  add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0, 
                  newdata = newdata_status, 
                  ndraws = 50) %>%   # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

newdata_dot_ind_prediction <- 
  add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0, 
                  newdata = newdata_sex_dot, 
                  ndraws = 50) %>%   # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

newdata_age_class_ind_prediction <- 
  add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0, 
                  newdata = newdata_age_class, 
                  ndraws = 50) %>%   # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

## derive inflection points:
# Extract posterior draws
post_age_class <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0)
post_sex <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0)
post_status <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0)
post_dot <- as_draws_df(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_dot_t0)

# Add combinations
post_age_class <- post_age_class %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_female = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_male   = b_baseline_Intercept + `b_baseline_sexM`,
    t0_JV  = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_AFY = b_t0_Intercept + `b_t0_age_classAFY`,
    inflection_score_female = 1 + (baseline_female - 1) / 2,
    inflection_score_male   = 1 + (baseline_male - 1) / 2
  )

post_sex <- post_sex %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_female = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_male   = b_baseline_Intercept + `b_baseline_sexM`,
    t0_F = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_M = b_t0_Intercept + `b_t0_sexM`,
    inflection_score_female = 1 + (baseline_female - 1) / 2,
    inflection_score_male   = 1 + (baseline_male - 1) / 2
  )

post_status <- post_status %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_female = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_male   = b_baseline_Intercept + `b_baseline_sexM`,
    t0_M  = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_R = b_t0_Intercept + `b_t0_migratory_statusR`,
    inflection_score_female = 1 + (baseline_female - 1) / 2,
    inflection_score_male   = 1 + (baseline_male - 1) / 2
  )

post_dot <- post_dot %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_female = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_male   = b_baseline_Intercept + `b_baseline_sexM`,
    t0_dot  = b_t0_Intercept,
    inflection_score_female = 1 + (baseline_female - 1) / 2,
    inflection_score_male   = 1 + (baseline_male - 1) / 2
  )

# Summarize
summary_inflections_age_class <- tibble(
  sex = c("F", "F", "M", "M"),
  age_class = c("FY", "AFY", "FY", "AFY"),
  group = c("Female-JV", "Female-AFY", "Male-JV", "Male-AFY"),
  t0 = c(mean(post_age_class$t0_JV), mean(post_age_class$t0_AFY), mean(post_age_class$t0_JV), mean(post_age_class$t0_AFY)),
  baseline = c(mean(post_age_class$baseline_female), mean(post_age_class$baseline_female), 
               mean(post_age_class$baseline_male), mean(post_age_class$baseline_male)),
  score = c(
    mean(post_age_class$inflection_score_female),
    mean(post_age_class$inflection_score_female),
    mean(post_age_class$inflection_score_male),
    mean(post_age_class$inflection_score_male)
  )
) %>% 
  mutate(t0_date_ = jday_to_date(t0 * date_J_sd + date_J_mean)) %>%
  mutate(
    # First, parse as if all dates are in 2022
    temp_date = dmy(paste(t0_date_, "2022")),
    
    # Then, assign actual year based on whether date is before Jan 1
    t0_date = if_else(
      temp_date < dmy("1 Jan 2022"),
      temp_date %m-% years(1),  # shift to 2021
      temp_date                # keep 2022
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-c(temp_date, t0_date_))  # optional: drop the helper column

summary_inflections_sex <- tibble(
  sex = c("F", "M"),
  group = c("Female", "Male"),
  t0 = c(mean(post_sex$t0_F), mean(post_sex$t0_M)),
  baseline = c(mean(post_age_class$baseline_female), mean(post_age_class$baseline_male)),
  score = c(
    mean(post_sex$inflection_score_female),
    mean(post_sex$inflection_score_male)
  )
) %>% 
  mutate(t0_date_ = jday_to_date(t0 * date_J_sd + date_J_mean)) %>%
  mutate(
    # First, parse as if all dates are in 2022
    temp_date = dmy(paste(t0_date_, "2022")),
    
    # Then, assign actual year based on whether date is before Jan 1
    t0_date = if_else(
      temp_date < dmy("1 Jan 2022"),
      temp_date %m-% years(1),  # shift to 2021
      temp_date                # keep 2022
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-c(temp_date, t0_date_))  # optional: drop the helper column

summary_inflections_status <- tibble(
  sex = c("F", "F", "M", "M"),
  status = c("M", "R", "M", "R"),
  group = c("Female-M", "Female-R", "Male-M", "Male-R"),
  t0 = c(mean(post_status$t0_M), mean(post_status$t0_R), mean(post_status$t0_M), mean(post_status$t0_R)),
  baseline = c(mean(post_age_class$baseline_female), mean(post_age_class$baseline_female), 
               mean(post_age_class$baseline_male), mean(post_age_class$baseline_male)),
  score = c(
    mean(post_status$inflection_score_female),
    mean(post_status$inflection_score_female),
    mean(post_status$inflection_score_male),
    mean(post_status$inflection_score_male)
  )
) %>% 
  mutate(t0_date_ = jday_to_date(t0 * date_J_sd + date_J_mean)) %>%
  mutate(
    # First, parse as if all dates are in 2022
    temp_date = dmy(paste(t0_date_, "2022")),
    
    # Then, assign actual year based on whether date is before Jan 1
    t0_date = if_else(
      temp_date < dmy("1 Jan 2022"),
      temp_date %m-% years(1),  # shift to 2021
      temp_date                # keep 2022
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-c(temp_date, t0_date_))  # optional: drop the helper column

summary_inflections_dot <- tibble(
  sex = c("F", "M"),
  group = c("Female", "Male"),
  t0 = c(mean(post_dot$t0_dot)),
  baseline = c(mean(post_age_class$baseline_female), mean(post_age_class$baseline_male)),
  score = c(
    mean(post_dot$inflection_score_female),
    mean(post_dot$inflection_score_male)
  )
) %>% 
  mutate(t0_date_ = jday_to_date(t0 * date_J_sd + date_J_mean)) %>%
  mutate(
    # First, parse as if all dates are in 2022
    temp_date = dmy(paste(t0_date_, "2022")),
    
    # Then, assign actual year based on whether date is before Jan 1
    t0_date = if_else(
      temp_date < dmy("1 Jan 2022"),
      temp_date %m-% years(1),  # shift to 2021
      temp_date                # keep 2022
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-c(temp_date, t0_date_))  # optional: drop the helper column

### plot curves ----
sex_pop_curves <-
  ggplot() +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = as.Date("2021-11-01"), xmax = max(found_dates_all_$date_),
                ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            fill = "grey70", alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(data = newdata_sex_ind_prediction, 
            aes(x = date_, y = .epred, color = sex,
                group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season)), alpha = 0.02) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = newdata_sex_pop_prediction %>% mutate(sex = factor(sex, levels = c("M", "F"))) %>% filter(sex == "M"),
    aes(x = date_, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = sex),
    alpha = 1,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  # geom_point(data = summary_inflections_sex %>% filter(sex == "M"), 
  #            aes(x = t0_date, y = score), 
  #            shape = 23, color = "black", fill = "white", size = 3) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = newdata_sex_pop_prediction %>% mutate(sex = factor(sex, levels = c("M", "F"))),
    aes(x = date_, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = sex),
    alpha = 1,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  # geom_point(data = summary_inflections_sex %>% filter(sex == "M"), 
  #            aes(x = t0_date, y = score), 
  #            shape = 23, color = "black", fill = "white", size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values =  c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values =  rev(c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 11, colour = "white"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"),
        # plot.margin = unit(c(-15,0.2,0.2,0.2), "cm"),
        panel.spacing = unit(1, "cm"),
        # panel.border = element_rect(size = 0.25, colour = "grey70", fill = 'transparent'),
        panel.border = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA), 
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = 'grey90', color = NA),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 13, colour = "grey40"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0, -15, 5.5, 0), "pt")) +
  scale_x_date(#date_labels = "%d %b", 
               expand = c(0.01, 0.01),
               #date_breaks = "2 month",
               #breaks = seq(c(as.Date("2021-11-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01"), by = 21)
               # date_breaks = "60 days",
               limits = c(as.Date("2021-11-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01")),
               breaks = seq(from = as.Date("2021-11-27"), to = as.Date("2022-03-12"), by = "21 days"), 
               date_labels = "%d %b") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5), breaks = seq(1, 7, 2)) +
  ylab("Rufous band score")# +
  # geom_point(data = summary_inflections_sex %>% filter(sex == "F"), 
  #            aes(x = t0_date, y = score), 
  #            shape = 23, color = "black", fill = "white", size = 3) +
  # geom_point(data = summary_inflections_sex, 
  #            aes(x = as.Date("2021-11-01"), y = baseline), 
  #            shape = 21, color = "black", fill = "white", size = 3)

        panel.border = element_blank(),

        plot.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA), 
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = 'grey90', color = NA),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 13, colour = "grey40"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0, -15, 5.5, 0), "pt")) +
  scale_x_date(#date_labels = "%d %b", 
               expand = c(0.01, 0.01),
               #date_breaks = "2 month",
               #breaks = seq(c(as.Date("2021-11-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01"), by = 21)
               # date_breaks = "60 days",
               limits = c(as.Date("2021-11-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01")),
               breaks = seq(from = as.Date("2021-11-27"), to = as.Date("2022-03-12"), by = "21 days"), 
               date_labels = "%d %b") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5), breaks = seq(1, 7, 2)) +
  ylab("Rufous band score")# +

status_pop_curves <-
  ggplot() +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = as.Date("2021-11-01"), xmax = max(found_dates_all_$date_),
                ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            fill = "grey70", alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(data = newdata_status_ind_prediction %>% filter(migratory_status == "R"), 
            aes(x = date_, y = .epred, #color = age_class,
                group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season)), color =  "#B07AA1", alpha = 0.02) +
  geom_line(data = newdata_status_ind_prediction %>% filter(migratory_status == "M"), 
            aes(x = date_, y = .epred, #color = age_class,
                group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season)), color =  "#76B7B2", alpha = 0.07) +
  # geom_line(data = newdata_status_pop_prediction,
  #           aes(x = date_, y = fit, group = interaction(sex, migratory_status), color = migratory_status), size = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = newdata_status_pop_prediction %>%
      mutate(migratory_status = factor(migratory_status,
                                       levels = c("M", "R")),
             sex = factor(sex, levels = c("M", "F"))) %>% filter(sex == "M"),
    aes(x = date_, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = migratory_status, group = interaction(sex, migratory_status)),
    alpha = 1,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  # geom_point(data = summary_inflections_status %>% filter(sex == "M"), 
  #            aes(x = t0_date, y = score, fill = status), 
  #            shape = 21, color = "white", size = 3) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = newdata_status_pop_prediction %>%
      mutate(migratory_status = factor(migratory_status,
                                       levels = c("M", "R")),
             sex = factor(sex, levels = c("M", "F"))) %>% filter(sex == "F"),
    aes(x = date_, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = migratory_status, group = interaction(sex, migratory_status)),
    alpha = 1,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  # geom_point(data = summary_inflections_status %>% filter(sex == "F"), 
  #            aes(x = t0_date, y = score, fill = status), 
  #            shape = 23, color = "white", size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values =  c("#76B7B2", "#B07AA1")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values =  c("#76B7B2", "#B07AA1")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 11, colour = "white"),
        # plot.margin = unit(c(-15,0.2,0.2,0.2), "cm"),
        panel.spacing = unit(1, "cm"),
        # panel.border = element_rect(size = 0.25, colour = "grey70", fill = 'transparent'),
        panel.border = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA), 
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = 'grey90', color = NA),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 13, colour = "grey40"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, 
                                   angle = 45, 
                                   hjust = 1, 
                                   vjust = 1, colour = "grey40"),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0, -15, 30, 0), "pt")) +
  scale_x_date(#date_labels = "%d %b", 
               expand = c(0.01, 0.01),
               #date_breaks = "2 month",
               #breaks = seq(c(as.Date("2021-11-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01"), by = 21)
               # date_breaks = "60 days",
               limits = c(as.Date("2021-11-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01")),
               breaks = seq(from = as.Date("2021-11-27"), to = as.Date("2022-03-12"), by = "21 days"), 
               date_labels = "%d %b") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5), breaks = seq(1, 7, 2)) +
  ylab("Rufous band score") +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill()# +
  # geom_point(data = summary_inflections_status %>% filter(status == "R"), 
  #            aes(x = as.Date("2021-11-01"), y = baseline, fill = sex), 
  #            shape = 21, color = "white", size = 3) +
  # scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))
  
final_plot2 <-
  (wrap_elements(all_pop_curves) +
   wrap_elements(sex_pop_curves) +
   wrap_elements(age_class_pop_curves) +
   wrap_elements(status_pop_curves)) + 
  plot_layout(ncol = 1,
    heights = c(1, 1, 1, 1.5)) & theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 5.5, 3))

raw_score_plot <-
  (nesting_hist / sex_score_plot) +
   plot_layout(heights = c(1, 3)) & theme(plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 0, 0))

# raw_score_plot_ <-
#   (wrap_elements(raw_score_plot) +
#      plot_spacer()) +
#   plot_layout(ncol = 2,
#     width = c(1.35, 0.65))
# 
# (wrap_elements(raw_score_plot) +
#     plot_spacer() +
#     wrap_elements(final_plot2) + 
#     wrap_elements(final_plot)) +
#   plot_layout(nrow = 2, ncol = 2,
#     widths = c(0.65, 1.35),
#     height = c(0.65, 1.35))

# First row: centered raw_score_plot
top_row <- wrap_elements(raw_score_plot) + 
  plot_spacer() +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.35, 0.65))

# Second row: final_plot2 and final_plot side-by-side
bottom_row <- wrap_elements(final_plot2) + 
  wrap_elements(final_plot) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(0.65, 1.35))

# Combine rows with heights
combined_plot <- top_row / bottom_row + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(0.8, 1.2))

ggsave(combined_plot,
       filename = "combined_plot.pdf",
       path = here("figs/"),
       device = cairo_pdf,
       width = 8, height = 12)

 # Print the final layout
combined_plot
```

### population-level curves

```{r}
dates_for_plot <- 
  data.frame(date_ = as.Date(c(as.Date("2021-07-01"):as.Date("2022-06-30"))), 
             date_J = c(1:365))

photo_data <- 
  dat %>% 
  filter(filename %in% c("IMG_0053.JPG", "IMG_0117.JPG", "IMG_0146.JPG", "IMG_0722.JPG", "IMG_2065.JPG", "IMG_0411.JPG")) %>% 
  select(rings_comb, sex, migratory_status, age_at_banding, age, score, date_J) %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J") %>% 
  mutate(score = as.numeric(score))

dat_ <- 
  dat %>%
  # classify score and sex
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(score),
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
    rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb),
    migratory_status = as.factor(migratory_status)
  ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>%
  # ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J)),
         date_J_sd = sd(date_J),
         date_J_mean = mean(date_J)) %>% 
  mutate(score = as.numeric(score))

# see what the mean and SD are for date_J
scaler <- scale(dat_$date_J)
date_mean <- attr(scaler, "scaled:center")  # mean
date_sd <- attr(scaler, "scaled:scale")   # SD

# Create new data for prediction
newdata_sex <- 
  expand.grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  sex = c("F", "M"),
  rings_comb = NA,   # exclude group-level effects
  season = NA
) %>% 
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")
                               
fitted_vals <- fitted(
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0,
  newdata = newdata_sex,
  re_formula = NA,  # exclude random effects
  summary = TRUE
)

newdata_sex$fit <- fitted_vals[, "Estimate"]
newdata_sex$lower <- fitted_vals[, "Q2.5"]
newdata_sex$upper <- fitted_vals[, "Q97.5"]

sex_pop_curves <-
  ggplot() +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = as.Date("2021-08-01"), xmax = as.Date("2021-12-31"),
                ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            fill = "grey70", alpha = 0.2) +  # adjust fill and alpha as needed +
  geom_jitter(data = dat_ %>% left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J"), 
              aes(x = date_, y = as.numeric(score), fill = sex), 
              shape = 21, size = 3, alpha = 0.2, 
              height = 0.15, width = 0) +
  geom_line(data = newdata_sex, 
            aes(x = date_, y = fit, color = sex), size = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = newdata_sex,
    aes(x = date_, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = sex),
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_point(data = photo_data %>% filter(score %in% c(5, 6)),
             aes(x = date_, y = score, fill = sex), 
             shape = 24, color = "white", size = 3, stroke = 1) +
  geom_point(data = photo_data %>% filter(score %in% c(4)),
             aes(x = date_, y = score, fill = sex), 
             shape = 22, color = "white", size = 3, stroke = 1) +
  geom_point(data = photo_data %>% filter(score %in% c(2)),
             aes(x = date_, y = score, fill = sex), 
             shape = 25, color = "white", size = 3, stroke = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 11, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        # plot.margin = unit(c(-15,0.2,0.2,0.2), "cm"),
        panel.spacing = unit(1, "cm"),
        panel.border = element_rect(size = 0.25, colour = "grey70", fill = 'transparent'),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA), 
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = 'grey90', color = NA),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 13, colour = "grey40"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, 
                                   angle = 45, 
                                   hjust = 1, 
                                   vjust = 1)) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%B", expand = c(0.01, 0.01),
               date_breaks = "2 month",
               limits = c(as.Date("2021-07-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01"))) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5), breaks = c(1:7)) +
  ylab("Rufous band score\n(mean ± 95% CI)") +
  annotate("text", 
         x = as.Date("2021-08-01") + (as.Date("2021-12-31") - as.Date("2021-08-01")) / 2,
         y = 1.15, 
         label = "Core nesting period",
         fontface = "italic",
         size = 3.5,  # adjust size as needed
         hjust = 0.5, 
         vjust = 0, colour = "grey40") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  # Add the double-headed arrow
  geom_segment(aes(x = as.Date("2021-08-01"), xend = as.Date("2021-12-31"), y = 1.5, yend = 1.5),
               arrow = arrow(ends = "both", type = "closed", length = unit(0.2, "cm")),
               linewidth = 0.8, color = "grey60")
```

### age-class multipanel figure

```{r}
dat_ <- 
  dat %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>% 
  # # classify score and sex
  # mutate(
  #   score = as.numeric(score),
  #   sex = as.factor(sex),
  #   season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
```
