```{r}
  #   migratory_status = as.factor(migratory_status)
  # ) %>%
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>%
  # ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J)),
         date_J_sd = sd(date_J),
         date_J_mean = mean(date_J))

# #### age_class ----
# # Clean data
# dat_age_class <- dat %>%
#   # remove outlier data
#   filter(date_J < 300) %>%
#   # classify score and sex
#   mutate(
#     score = as.numeric(score),
#     sex = as.factor(sex),
#     season = factor(season, levels = c("2021", "2022", "2023")),
#     rings_comb = as.factor(rings_comb),
#     age_class = factor(ifelse(age == 1, "FY", "AFY"), levels = c("FY", "AFY"))
#   ) %>%
#   # scale the date_J variable
#   group_by(season) %>% 
#   mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J))) %>% 
#   ungroup() %>%
#   # drop NAs
#   drop_na(score, date_J, sex, age, age_class, rings_comb, season) %>% 
#   # reduce to distinct data
#   distinct() %>%
#   # convert to dataframe
#   as.data.frame()

dates_for_plot <- 
  data.frame(date_ = as.Date(c(as.Date("2021-07-01"):as.Date("2022-06-30"))), 
             date_J = c(1:365))

# Create new data for prediction
newdata_age_class_pop_prediction <- 
  expand.grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  sex = c("F", "M"),
  age_class = c("FY", "AFY"),
  rings_comb = NA,   # exclude group-level effects
  season = NA
) %>% 
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

fitted_vals <- fitted(
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0,
  newdata = newdata_age_class_pop_prediction,
  re_formula = NA,  # exclude random effects
  summary = TRUE
)

newdata_age_class_pop_prediction$fit <- fitted_vals[, "Estimate"]
newdata_age_class_pop_prediction$lower <- fitted_vals[, "Q2.5"]
newdata_age_class_pop_prediction$upper <- fitted_vals[, "Q97.5"]

# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex_age_class <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0$data %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex, age_class)

# Expand grid for predictions
newdata_age_class <- expand_grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  id_season_sex_age_class
)

# Get predicted curves with uncertainty
newdata_age_class_ind_prediction <- 
  add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0, 
                  newdata = newdata_age_class, 
                  ndraws = 50) %>%   # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

age_pop_curves <-
  ggplot() +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = as.Date("2021-08-01"), xmax = as.Date("2021-12-31"),
                ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            fill = "grey70", alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(data = newdata_age_class_ind_prediction %>% filter(age_class == "AFY"), 
            aes(x = date_, y = .epred, #color = age_class,
                group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season)), color =  "#59A14F", alpha = 0.02) +
  geom_line(data = newdata_age_class_ind_prediction %>% filter(age_class == "FY"), 
            aes(x = date_, y = .epred, #color = age_class,
                group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season)), color =  "#E15759", alpha = 0.07) +  # adjust fill and alpha as needed +
  # scale_color_manual(values = c("#E15759", "#59A14F")) +
  # ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +
  # geom_jitter(data = dat_age_class %>% left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J"), 
  #             aes(x = date_, y = as.numeric(score), fill = age_class, shape = age_class), 
  #             # shape = 21, 
  #             size = 3, alpha = 0.2, 
  #             height = 0.15, width = 0) +
  geom_line(data = newdata_age_class_pop_prediction, 
            aes(x = date_, y = fit, group = interaction(sex, age_class), color = age_class), size = 1.2) +
  # geom_ribbon(
  #   data = newdata_age_class,
  #   aes(x = date_, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, group = interaction(sex, age_class), fill = age_class),
  #   alpha = 0.2,
  #   color = NA
  # ) +
  # scale_fill_manual(values = c("#A84042", "#3E7237")) +
  # scale_color_manual(values = c("#A84042", "#3E7237")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values =  c("#E15759", "#59A14F")) +
  scale_color_manual(values =  c("#E15759", "#59A14F")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 11, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        # plot.margin = unit(c(-15,0.2,0.2,0.2), "cm"),
        panel.spacing = unit(1, "cm"),
        panel.border = element_rect(size = 0.25, colour = "grey70", fill = 'transparent'),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA), 
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = 'grey90', color = NA),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 13, colour = "grey40"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, 
                                   angle = 45, 
                                   hjust = 1, 
                                   vjust = 1),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0, 5.5, 5.5, 0), "pt")) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%B", expand = c(0.01, 0.01),
               date_breaks = "1 month",
               # date_breaks = "60 days",
               limits = c(as.Date("2021-07-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01"))) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5), breaks = c(1:7)) +
  ylab("Rufous band score\n(mean ± 95% CI)")# +
  # annotate("text", 
  #        x = as.Date("2021-08-01") + (as.Date("2021-12-31") - as.Date("2021-08-01")) / 2,
  #        y = 1.15, 
  #        label = "Core nesting period",
  #        fontface = "italic",
  #        size = 3.5,  # adjust size as needed
  #        hjust = 0.5, 
  #        vjust = 0, colour = "grey40") +
  # scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Spectral") +
  # scale_color_brewer(palette = "Spectral") +
  # Add the double-headed arrow
  # geom_segment(aes(x = as.Date("2021-08-01"), xend = as.Date("2021-12-31"), y = 1.5, yend = 1.5),
  #              arrow = arrow(ends = "both", type = "closed", length = unit(0.2, "cm")),
  #              linewidth = 0.8, color = "grey60")

age_class_baseline_post_plot <-
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c(
           "baseline_migratory_statusM", "baseline_migratory_statusR", 
           "baseline_sexF", "baseline_sexM", 
           "baseline_age_classFY", "baseline_age_classAFY"
         )) %>% 
         filter(model == "age class"), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term, fill = term)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    aes(height = after_stat(density)),  # REQUIRED when stat = "density"
    stat = "density",
    scale = -3.6,                       # Negative = upside-down
    rel_min_height = 0.0001, color = NA
  ) +
  geom_errorbarh(data = posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("baseline_migratory_statusM", "baseline_migratory_statusR", 
           "baseline_sexF", "baseline_sexM", 
           "baseline_age_classFY", "baseline_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "age class") %>%
            group_by(term, model) %>%
            summarise(
              median = median(estimate),
              lower = quantile(estimate, 0.025),
              upper = quantile(estimate, 0.975),
              .groups = "drop"), aes(y = term, x = median, xmin = lower, xmax = upper), color = "white", height = 1) +
  geom_point(data = posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("baseline_migratory_statusM", "baseline_migratory_statusR", 
           "baseline_sexF", "baseline_sexM", 
           "baseline_age_classFY", "baseline_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "age class") %>%
            group_by(term, model) %>%
            summarise(
              median = median(estimate),
              lower = quantile(estimate, 0.025),
              upper = quantile(estimate, 0.975),
              .groups = "drop"), size = 2, shape = 21, color = "black", fill = "white", aes(y = term, x = median)) +
  # geom_density_ridges(
  #   stat = "density",
  #   aes(height = after_stat(density) * -1),
  #   scale = 0.6,              # REDUCED SCALE
  #   rel_min_height = 0        # Prevent ridge clipping
  # ) +
  # stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), color = "black", point_fill = "white", shape = 21, slab_alpha = 0.75, aes(slab_y = -after_stat(density))) +
  labs(x = "Rufous band score prior to pre-basic moult", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0, 5.5, -5.5, 5.5), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "baseline_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "baseline_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "baseline_sexF" = "Female",
  "baseline_sexM" = "Male",
  "baseline_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "baseline_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5), breaks = c(1:7)) +
  # scale_x_continuous(
  #   limits = c(4, 7),
  #   breaks = seq(4, 7, by = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  coord_flip()

age_class_t0_post_plot <-
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "age class"), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term, fill = term)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    aes(height = after_stat(density)),  # REQUIRED when stat = "density"
    stat = "density",
    scale = 3.6,                       # Negative = upside-down
    rel_min_height = 0.001, color = NA
  ) +
  geom_errorbarh(data = posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "age class") %>%
            group_by(term, model) %>%
            summarise(
              median = median(estimate),
              lower = quantile(estimate, 0.025),
              upper = quantile(estimate, 0.975),
              .groups = "drop"), aes(y = term, x = median, xmin = lower, xmax = upper), color = "white", height = 1) +
  geom_point(data = posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "age class") %>%
            group_by(term, model) %>%
            summarise(
              median = median(estimate),
              lower = quantile(estimate, 0.025),
              upper = quantile(estimate, 0.975),
              .groups = "drop"), size = 2, shape = 21, color = "black", fill = "white", aes(y = term, x = median)) +
  # stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), color = "black", point_fill = "white", shape = 21, slab_alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(x = expression("Inflection point estimate of pre-basic moult (" * t[0] * ")"), y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0, 5.5, -5.5, 5.5), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "t0_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "t0_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "t0_sexF" = "Female",
  "t0_sexM" = "Male",
  "t0_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "t0_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
  # scale_x_date(date_labels = "%B", expand = c(0.01, 0.01),
  #              date_breaks = "2 month",
  #              limits = c(as.Date("2021-07-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01"))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(1, 305),
    breaks = seq(1, 305, by = 60), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +#,  # or choose your preferred ticks
    # labels = jday_to_date) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#59A14F", "#E15759"))

# Combine with patchwork
layout <- "AB
           CD"

age_class_full_plot <- 
  plot_spacer() + age_class_t0_post_plot + age_class_baseline_post_plot + age_pop_curves +
  plot_layout(design = layout,
    widths = c(0.5, 3),    # First column (A/C) is narrow, second column (B/D) is wider
    heights = c(0.5, 3)    # First row (A/B) is shorter, second row (C/D) is taller
  ) +
  plot_annotation(theme = theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0)))
```

### sex multipanel figure

```{r}
fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0 <- readRDS(here("out/fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0.rds"))

dat_ <- 
  dat %>%
  # remove outlier data
  filter(date_J < 300) %>% 
  # # classify score and sex
  # scale the date_J variable
  group_by(season) %>%
  # ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(date_J_scaled = as.vector(scale(date_J)),
         date_J_sd = sd(date_J),
         date_J_mean = mean(date_J))

dates_for_plot <- 
  data.frame(date_ = as.Date(c(as.Date("2021-07-01"):as.Date("2022-06-30"))), 
             date_J = c(1:365))

# Create new data for prediction
newdata_sex_pop_prediction <- 
  expand.grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  sex = c("F", "M"),
  rings_comb = NA,   # exclude group-level effects
  season = NA
) %>% 
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

fitted_vals <- fitted(
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0,
  newdata = newdata_sex_pop_prediction,
  re_formula = NA,  # exclude random effects
  summary = TRUE
)

newdata_sex_pop_prediction$fit <- fitted_vals[, "Estimate"]
newdata_sex_pop_prediction$lower <- fitted_vals[, "Q2.5"]
newdata_sex_pop_prediction$upper <- fitted_vals[, "Q97.5"]

# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0$data %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex)

# Expand grid for predictions
newdata_age_class <- expand_grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  id_season_sex
)

# Get predicted curves with uncertainty
newdata_sex_ind_prediction <- 
  add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_t0, 
                  newdata = newdata_age_class, 
                  ndraws = 50) %>%   # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves
  left_join(., data.frame(date_J = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J),
                                      to = max(dat_$date_J),
                                      length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
                         date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
                                             length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1)),
            by = "date_J_scaled") %>% 
  left_join(., dates_for_plot, by = "date_J")

sex_pop_curves <-
  ggplot() +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = as.Date("2021-08-01"), xmax = as.Date("2021-12-31"),
                ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            fill = "grey70", alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(data = newdata_sex_ind_prediction, 
            aes(x = date_, y = .epred, color = sex,
                group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season)), alpha = 0.02) +
  geom_line(data = newdata_sex_pop_prediction, 
            aes(x = date_, y = fit, group = interaction(sex, sex), color = sex), size = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values =  c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(family = "Lato"),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 11, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
        # plot.margin = unit(c(-15,0.2,0.2,0.2), "cm"),
        panel.spacing = unit(1, "cm"),
        panel.border = element_rect(size = 0.25, colour = "grey70", fill = 'transparent'),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', color = NA), 
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = 'grey90', color = NA),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 13, colour = "grey40"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(size = 0.25, lineend = "round", colour = "grey90"),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, 
                                   angle = 45, 
                                   hjust = 1, 
                                   vjust = 1),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0, 5.5, 5.5, 0), "pt")) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%B", expand = c(0.01, 0.01),
               date_breaks = "1 month",
               # date_breaks = "60 days",
               limits = c(as.Date("2021-07-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01"))) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5), breaks = c(1:7)) +
  ylab("Rufous band score\n(mean ± 95% CI)")

sex_baseline_post_plot <-
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c(
           "baseline_migratory_statusM", "baseline_migratory_statusR", 
           "baseline_sexF", "baseline_sexM", 
           "baseline_age_classFY", "baseline_age_classAFY"
         )) %>% 
         filter(model == "sex"), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term, fill = term)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    aes(height = after_stat(density)),  # REQUIRED when stat = "density"
    stat = "density",
    scale = -3.6,                       # Negative = upside-down
    rel_min_height = 0.0001, color = NA
  ) +
  geom_errorbarh(data = posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("baseline_migratory_statusM", "baseline_migratory_statusR", 
           "baseline_sexF", "baseline_sexM", 
           "baseline_age_classFY", "baseline_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "sex") %>%
            group_by(term, model) %>%
            summarise(
              median = median(estimate),
              lower = quantile(estimate, 0.025),
              upper = quantile(estimate, 0.975),
              .groups = "drop"), aes(y = term, x = median, xmin = lower, xmax = upper), color = "white", height = 1) +
  geom_point(data = posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("baseline_migratory_statusM", "baseline_migratory_statusR", 
           "baseline_sexF", "baseline_sexM", 
           "baseline_age_classFY", "baseline_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "sex") %>%
            group_by(term, model) %>%
            summarise(
              median = median(estimate),
              lower = quantile(estimate, 0.025),
              upper = quantile(estimate, 0.975),
              .groups = "drop"), size = 2, shape = 21, color = "black", fill = "white", aes(y = term, x = median)) +
  # geom_density_ridges(
  #   stat = "density",
  #   aes(height = after_stat(density) * -1),
  #   scale = 0.6,              # REDUCED SCALE
  #   rel_min_height = 0        # Prevent ridge clipping
  # ) +
  # stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), color = "black", point_fill = "white", shape = 21, slab_alpha = 0.75, aes(slab_y = -after_stat(density))) +
  labs(x = "Rufous band score prior to pre-basic moult", y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0, 5.5, -5.5, 5.5), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "baseline_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "baseline_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "baseline_sexF" = "Female",
  "baseline_sexM" = "Male",
  "baseline_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "baseline_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5), breaks = c(1:7)) +
  # scale_x_continuous(
  #   limits = c(4, 7),
  #   breaks = seq(4, 7, by = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  coord_flip()

sex_t0_post_plot <-
  ggplot(posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "sex"), 
       aes(x = estimate, y = term, fill = term)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    aes(height = after_stat(density)),  # REQUIRED when stat = "density"
    stat = "density",
    scale = 3.6,                       # Negative = upside-down
    rel_min_height = 0.001, color = NA
  ) +
  geom_errorbarh(data = posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "sex") %>%
            group_by(term, model) %>%
            summarise(
              median = median(estimate),
              lower = quantile(estimate, 0.025),
              upper = quantile(estimate, 0.975),
              .groups = "drop"), aes(y = term, x = median, xmin = lower, xmax = upper), color = "white", height = 1) +
  geom_point(data = posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "sex") %>%
            group_by(term, model) %>%
            summarise(
              median = median(estimate),
              lower = quantile(estimate, 0.025),
              upper = quantile(estimate, 0.975),
              .groups = "drop"), size = 2, shape = 21, color = "black", fill = "white", aes(y = term, x = median)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_c() +
  geom_density_ridges(
    aes(height = after_stat(density)),  # REQUIRED when stat = "density"
    stat = "density",
    scale = 3.6,                       # Negative = upside-down
    rel_min_height = 0.001, color = NA
  ) +
  geom_errorbarh(data = posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "sex") %>%
            group_by(term, model) %>%
            summarise(
              median = median(estimate),
              lower = quantile(estimate, 0.025),
              upper = quantile(estimate, 0.975),
              .groups = "drop"), aes(y = term, x = median, xmin = lower, xmax = upper), color = "white", height = 1) +
  geom_point(data = posterior_long %>% 
         filter(term %in% c("t0_migratory_statusM", "t0_migratory_statusR", 
                            "t0_sexF", "t0_sexM", 
                            "t0_age_classFY", "t0_age_classAFY")) %>% 
           filter(model == "sex") %>%
            group_by(term, model) %>%
            summarise(
              median = median(estimate),
              lower = quantile(estimate, 0.025),
              upper = quantile(estimate, 0.975),
              .groups = "drop"), size = 2, shape = 21, color = "black", fill = "white", aes(y = term, x = median)) +
  # stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95), color = "black", point_fill = "white", shape = 21, slab_alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(x = expression("Inflection point estimate of pre-basic moult (" * t[0] * ")"), y = "Parameter") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0, 5.5, -5.5, 5.5), "pt")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
  "t0_migratory_statusM" = "Migrant",
  "t0_migratory_statusR" = "Resident",
  "t0_sexF" = "Female",
  "t0_sexM" = "Male",
  "t0_age_classFY" = "First-year",
  "t0_age_classAFY" = "Adult")) + 
  facet_grid(model ~ ., labeller = labeller(model = c(
  "age class" = "Age-class model",
  "sex" = "Sex-specific model",
  "migratory status" = "Migratory strategy model"
))) +
  # scale_x_date(date_labels = "%B", expand = c(0.01, 0.01),
  #              date_breaks = "2 month",
  #              limits = c(as.Date("2021-07-01"), as.Date("2022-05-01"))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(1, 305),
    breaks = seq(1, 305, by = 60), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) #+#,  # or choose your preferred ticks
    # labels = jday_to_date)

# Combine with patchwork
layout <- "AB
           CD"

sex_full_plot <- 
  plot_spacer() + sex_t0_post_plot + sex_baseline_post_plot + sex_pop_curves +
  plot_layout(design = layout,
    widths = c(0.5, 3),    # First column (A/C) is narrow, second column (B/D) is wider
    heights = c(0.5, 3)    # First row (A/B) is shorter, second row (C/D) is taller
  ) +
  plot_annotation(theme = theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0)))
```

```{r}
# Extract unique combinations of grouping variables
id_season_sex_age_class <- 
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0$data %>%
  distinct(rings_comb, season, sex, age_class)

# Expand grid for predictions
newdata <- expand_grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               to = max(dat_$date_J_scaled),
               length.out = max(dat_$date_J) - min(dat_$date_J) + 1),
  id_season_sex_age_class
)

# Get predicted curves with uncertainty
newdata_age_class_ind_prediction <- 
  add_epred_draws(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_age_class_t0, 
                  newdata = newdata, 
                  ndraws = 100)  # You can increase `ndraws` for smoother curves

preds %>%
  filter(is.na(date_J_scaled) | is.na(.epred) | is.na(rings_comb))

ggplot(newdata_age_class_ind_prediction, 
       aes(x = date_J_scaled, y = .epred, color = age_class,
           group = interaction(rings_comb, .draw, season))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.02) +
  # facet_wrap(rings_comb ~ .) +
  labs(title = "Individual curves across seasons by sex", y = "Predicted score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#F28E2B", "#4E79A7"))

preds %>% ungroup() %>% filter(rings_comb == "RRGO") %>% select(rings_comb, sex) %>% distinct()
  
#### status ----
# Create new data for prediction
newdata_status <- expand.grid(
  date_J_scaled = seq(from = min(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0$data$date_J_scaled),
                      to = max(fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0$data$date_J_scaled),
                      length.out = 100),
  sex = c("F", "M"),
  migratory_status = c("R", "M"),
  rings_comb = NA,   # exclude group-level effects
  season = NA
) %>% 
  mutate(date_J = date_J_scaled * date_sd + date_mean)

fitted_vals <- fitted(
  fit_brm_cor_re_sex_baseline_status_t0,
  newdata = newdata_status,
  re_formula = NA,  # exclude random effects
  summary = TRUE
)

newdata_status$fit <- fitted_vals[, "Estimate"]
newdata_status$lower <- fitted_vals[, "Q2.5"]
newdata_status$upper <- fitted_vals[, "Q97.5"]

status_pop_curves <-
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = newdata_status, 
            aes(x = date_J, y = fit, group = interaction(sex, migratory_status), color = migratory_status), 
            size = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(data = newdata_status, 
              aes(x = date_J, y = fit, group = interaction(sex, migratory_status), fill = migratory_status, 
                  ymin = lower, ymax = upper), 
              alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "Predicted Score",
    title = "Population-level predicted curves by migratory status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


### to remove:
#### sex-specific phenology model: last date breed
```{r}
breed_vars <- sex_last_date_breed_mod$data %>%
  select(
    rings_comb,
    season,
    last_breeding_date_J_snum,
    first_breeding_date_J_snum,
    last_nest_first_obs_J_snum
  ) %>%
  distinct()

# library(broom)

pairs <- list(
  c("last_breeding_date_J_snum", "first_breeding_date_J_snum"),
  c("last_breeding_date_J_snum", "last_nest_first_obs_J_snum"),
  c("first_breeding_date_J_snum", "last_nest_first_obs_J_snum")
)

cor_tests <- lapply(pairs, function(p) {
  ct <- cor.test(
    breed_vars[[p[1]]],
    breed_vars[[p[2]]],
    method = "pearson"
  )
  broom::tidy(ct) %>%
    mutate(var1 = p[1], var2 = p[2])
})

cor_results <- bind_rows(cor_tests) %>%
  select(var1, var2, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)

cor_results

# library(car)

vif(lm(
  last_breeding_date_J_snum ~ 
    first_breeding_date_J_snum + last_nest_first_obs_J_snum,
  data = breed_vars
))

vif(lm(
  first_breeding_date_J_snum ~ 
    last_breeding_date_J_snum + last_nest_first_obs_J_snum,
  data = breed_vars
))

vif(lm(
  last_nest_first_obs_J_snum ~ 
    last_breeding_date_J_snum + first_breeding_date_J_snum,
  data = breed_vars
))

# ================================
# 2. Extract posterior draws
# ================================
post <- as_draws_df(sex_last_date_breed_mod$fitted_model)

# ----------------
# Standardized sex effect on baseline
resid_sd <- sd(sex_last_date_breed_mod$data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd
sex_t0_std <- post$b_t0_sexM / resid_sd

lay_sd <- 
  round(sd(sex_last_date_breed_mod$data$last_breeding_date_J_snum, na.rm = TRUE))

# ----------------
# Back-transform last_breeding_date_J_snum effect to "per 10 days"
# Multiply by mean_date_sd to go from standardized units to days
# Then multiply by 10 to express per 10 days

last_breed_scaling <- sex_last_date_breed_mod$data %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_J = mean(last_breeding_date_J_snum),
      sd_J   = sd(last_breeding_date_J_snum),
      n      = dplyr::n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

# Weighted mean SD across seasons (recommended)
mean_date_sd   <- with(last_breed_scaling, weighted.mean(sd_J, w = n))
mean_date_mean <- with(last_breed_scaling, weighted.mean(mean_J, w = n))

# last_date_baseline_SDd <- post$b_baseline_last_breeding_date_J_snum * mean_date_sd * lay_sd
last_date_t0_SDd       <- post$b_t0_last_breeding_date_J_snum * mean_date_sd

# Posterior summaries
# last_date_baseline_SDd_summary <- summarize_posterior(last_date_baseline_SDd)
last_date_t0_SDd_summary       <- summarize_posterior(last_date_t0_SDd)

# ----------------
# Compute sex-specific baseline medians and 95% CI
baseline_sex_summary <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_F = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_M = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    median_F     = median(baseline_F),
    ci_lower_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.025),
    ci_upper_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.975),
    median_M     = median(baseline_M),
    ci_lower_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.025),
    ci_upper_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.975)
  )

# ================================
# 3. Back-transform t0 and baseline
# ================================
date_scaling <- sex_last_date_breed_mod$data %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_J = mean(date_J),
      sd_J   = sd(date_J),
      n      = dplyr::n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

# Weighted mean SD across seasons (recommended)
mean_date_sd   <- with(date_scaling, weighted.mean(sd_J, w = n))
mean_date_mean <- with(date_scaling, weighted.mean(mean_J, w = n))

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # sex-specific baseline
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,

    # t0 is NOT sex-specific in this model
    t0_sexF_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_sexM_scaled = b_t0_Intercept + b_t0_sexM,
    t0_sexF = t0_sexF_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean,
    t0_sexM = t0_sexM_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean
  )

# ================================
# 4. Posterior summary helper
# ================================
summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    median   = median(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# ----------------
# Summarize key fixed effects
std_sex_baseline_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_baseline_std)
std_sex_t0_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_t0_std)
t0_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$t0_sexF)
t0_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$t0_sexM)
baseline_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$baseline_sexF)
baseline_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$baseline_sexM)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
t0_sexF_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_sexF_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01")) %>% 
  format(., "%b-%d")

t0_sexM_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_sexM_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01")) %>% 
  format(., "%b-%d")

t0_sexM_summary_trans
t0_sexF_summary_trans

# ================================
# 5. Interpret k: peak rate of change in days·score⁻¹
# ================================
date_sd <- mean_date_sd

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # Peak rate in scores·day⁻¹
    rate_scores_day_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_scores_day_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    # Convert to days·score⁻¹
    rate_days_score_sexF = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexF,
    rate_days_score_sexM = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexM
  )

rate_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexF)
rate_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexM)

# ================================
# 6. Bayesian R²
# ================================
r2_df <- r2_bayes(sex_last_date_breed_mod$fitted_model) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_summary <- tibble(
  type     = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
  mean     = c(r2_df$R2[2], r2_df$R2[1]),
  ci_lower = c(r2_df$CI_low[2], r2_df$CI_low[1]),
  ci_upper = c(r2_df$CI_high[2], r2_df$CI_high[1])
)

# ================================
# 7. Variance components & repeatabilities
# ================================
post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    var_baseline_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2,
    var_t0_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2,
    R_baseline = var_baseline_indiv / (var_baseline_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_t0       = var_t0_indiv / (var_t0_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_baseline_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2),
    R_t0_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2)
  )

R_baseline_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline)
R_t0_summary                     <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0)
R_baseline_within_season_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline_within_season)
R_t0_within_season_summary       <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0_within_season)

# ================================
# 8. Variance component table
# ================================
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2
)

var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2
)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

# ================================
# 11. Posterior correlation between baseline and t0
# ================================
cor_baseline_t0_summary_ <- summarize_posterior(post$cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept)

### summary table 
sex_last_date_breed_mod_summary_table <- tibble(
  parameter = c(
    "Female median alternate plumage (beta)",
    "Male median alternate plumage (beta)",
    "Female pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Male pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Effect of a 1-month shift in last breeding date on moult mid-point date",
    "Female moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "Male moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "Marginal R²",
    "Conditional R²",
    "Random effect variance (baseline)",
    "Random effect variance (t0)",
    "Correlation between beta and t0",
    "Residual variance"
  ),
  type = c(
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Model-level goodness-of-fit",
    "Model-level goodness-of-fit",
    "Between-individual variance (random effects)",
    "Between-individual variance (random effects)",
    "Random-effects correlation",
    "Residual (within-observation) variance"
  ),
  parameter_ = c(
    "Full alternate plumage score (beta)",
    "Full alternate plumage score (beta)",
    "Pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Breeding phenology",
    "Moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "Moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "",
    "",
    "",
    "",
    "",
    ""
  ),
  mean = c(
    baseline_sex_summary$median_F %>% round(2),
    baseline_sex_summary$median_M %>% round(2),
    t0_sexF_summary_trans["median"],
    t0_sexM_summary_trans["median"],
    last_date_t0_SDd_summary["median"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexF_summary["median"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexM_summary["median"] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$mean[1] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$mean[2] %>% round(2),
    var_baseline_rings_comb["median"] %>% round(2),
    var_t0_rings_comb["median"] %>% round(2),
    cor_baseline_t0_summary_["median"] %>% round(2),
    resid_var["median"] %>% round(2)
  ),
  ci_lower = c(
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_F %>% round(2),
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_M %>% round(2),
    t0_sexF_summary_trans["ci_lower.2.5%"],
    t0_sexM_summary_trans["ci_lower.2.5%"],
    last_date_t0_SDd_summary["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_lower[1] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_lower[2] %>% round(2),
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    cor_baseline_t0_summary_["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    resid_var["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2)
  ),
  ci_upper = c(
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_F %>% round(2),
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_M %>% round(2),
    t0_sexF_summary_trans["ci_upper.97.5%"],
    t0_sexM_summary_trans["ci_upper.97.5%"],
    last_date_t0_SDd_summary["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_upper[1] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_upper[2] %>% round(2),
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    cor_baseline_t0_summary_["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    resid_var["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2)
  )
) %>%
  mutate(median_CI = paste0(mean, " [", ci_lower, ", ", ci_upper, "]"))

sex_last_date_breed_mod_summary_table
# ================================
# 9. Posterior distributions of fixed effects
# ================================
posterior_fixed <- sex_last_date_breed_mod$fitted_model %>%
  spread_draws(
    b_baseline_Intercept,
    b_baseline_sexM,
    b_baseline_last_breeding_date_J_snum,
    b_t0_Intercept,
    b_t0_sexM,
    b_t0_last_breeding_date_J_snum,
    b_k_Intercept
  )

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>%
  select(-c(.chain, .iteration, .draw)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),
               fill = "skyblue",
               color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
    x = "Estimate",
    y = "Parameter"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# # ================================
# # 10. Access key derived quantities
# # ================================
# post$baseline_sexF
# post$baseline_sexM
# post$t0
# post$rate_days_score_sexF
# post$rate_days_score_sexM

# ================================
# 12. Posterior predictive checks
# ================================
# Plot observed vs simulated data
pp_check(sex_last_date_breed_mod$fitted_model, ndraws = 100)

# ================================
# 13. R-hat checks for convergence
# ================================
rhat_values <- posterior::rhat(sex_last_date_breed_mod$fitted_model)
summary(rhat_values)  # All should be ~1 for good convergence
```

#### sex-specific phenology model: first date breed
```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Extract posterior draws
# ================================
post <- as_draws_df(sex_first_date_breed_mod$fitted_model)

# ----------------
# Standardized sex effect on baseline
resid_sd <- sd(sex_first_date_breed_mod$data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd
sex_t0_std <- post$b_t0_sexM / resid_sd

lay_sd <- round(sd(sex_first_date_breed_mod$data$first_breeding_date_J_snum, na.rm = TRUE))

# ----------------
# Back-transform first_breeding_date_J_snum effect to "per 10 days"
# Multiply by mean_date_sd to go from standardized units to days
# Then multiply by 10 to express per 10 days

first_date_baseline_SDd <- post$b_baseline_first_breeding_date_J_snum * mean_date_sd * lay_sd
first_date_t0_SDd       <- post$b_t0_first_breeding_date_J_snum * mean_date_sd * lay_sd

# Posterior summaries
first_date_baseline_SDd_summary <- summarize_posterior(first_date_baseline_SDd)
first_date_t0_SDd_summary       <- summarize_posterior(first_date_t0_SDd)

# ----------------
# Compute sex-specific baseline medians and 95% CI
baseline_sex_summary <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_F = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_M = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    median_F     = median(baseline_F),
    ci_lower_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.025),
    ci_upper_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.975),
    median_M     = median(baseline_M),
    ci_lower_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.025),
    ci_upper_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.975)
  )

# ================================
# 3. Back-transform t0 and baseline
# ================================
date_scaling <- sex_first_date_breed_mod$data %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_J = mean(date_J),
      sd_J   = sd(date_J),
      n      = dplyr::n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

# Weighted mean SD across seasons (recommended)
mean_date_sd   <- with(date_scaling, weighted.mean(sd_J, w = n))
mean_date_mean <- with(date_scaling, weighted.mean(mean_J, w = n))

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # sex-specific baseline
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,

    # t0 is NOT sex-specific in this model
    t0_sexF_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_sexM_scaled = b_t0_Intercept + b_t0_sexM,
    t0_sexF = t0_sexF_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean,
    t0_sexM = t0_sexM_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean
  )

# ================================
# 4. Posterior summary helper
# ================================
summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    median   = median(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# ----------------
# Summarize key fixed effects
std_sex_baseline_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_baseline_std)
std_sex_t0_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_t0_std)
t0_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$t0_sexF)
t0_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$t0_sexM)
baseline_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$baseline_sexF)
baseline_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$baseline_sexM)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
t0_sexF_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_sexF_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01")) %>% 
  format(., "%b-%d")

t0_sexM_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_sexM_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01")) %>% 
  format(., "%b-%d")

t0_sexM_summary_trans
t0_sexF_summary_trans

# ================================
# 5. Interpret k: peak rate of change in days·score⁻¹
# ================================
date_sd <- mean_date_sd

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # Peak rate in scores·day⁻¹
    rate_scores_day_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_scores_day_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    # Convert to days·score⁻¹
    rate_days_score_sexF = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexF,
    rate_days_score_sexM = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexM
  )

rate_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexF)
rate_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexM)

# ================================
# 6. Bayesian R²
# ================================
r2_df <- r2_bayes(sex_first_date_breed_mod$fitted_model) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_summary <- tibble(
  type     = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
  mean     = c(r2_df$R2[2], r2_df$R2[1]),
  ci_lower = c(r2_df$CI_low[2], r2_df$CI_low[1]),
  ci_upper = c(r2_df$CI_high[2], r2_df$CI_high[1])
)

# ================================
# 7. Variance components & repeatabilities
# ================================
post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    var_baseline_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2,
    var_t0_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2,
    R_baseline = var_baseline_indiv / (var_baseline_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_t0       = var_t0_indiv / (var_t0_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_baseline_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2),
    R_t0_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2)
  )

R_baseline_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline)
R_t0_summary                     <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0)
R_baseline_within_season_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline_within_season)
R_t0_within_season_summary       <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0_within_season)

# ================================
# 8. Variance component table
# ================================
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2
)

var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2
)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

# ================================
# 11. Posterior correlation between baseline and t0
# ================================
cor_baseline_t0_summary_ <- summarize_posterior(post$cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept)

### summary table 
sex_first_date_breed_mod_summary_table <- tibble(
  parameter = c(
    "Female median alternate plumage (beta)",
    "Male median alternate plumage (beta)",
    "Female pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Male pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Effect of a 1-month shift in first breeding date on moult mid-point date",
    "Female moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "Male moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "Marginal R²",
    "Conditional R²",
    "Random effect variance (baseline)",
    "Random effect variance (t0)",
    "Correlation between beta and t0",
    "Residual variance"
  ),
  type = c(
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Model-level goodness-of-fit",
    "Model-level goodness-of-fit",
    "Between-individual variance (random effects)",
    "Between-individual variance (random effects)",
    "Random-effects correlation",
    "Residual (within-observation) variance"
  ),
  parameter_ = c(
    "Full alternate plumage score (beta)",
    "Full alternate plumage score (beta)",
    "Pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Breeding phenology",
    "Moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "Moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "",
    "",
    "",
    "",
    "",
    ""
  ),
  mean = c(
    baseline_sex_summary$median_F %>% round(2),
    baseline_sex_summary$median_M %>% round(2),
    t0_sexF_summary_trans["median"],
    t0_sexM_summary_trans["median"],
    first_date_t0_SDd_summary["median"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexF_summary["median"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexM_summary["median"] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$mean[1] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$mean[2] %>% round(2),
    var_baseline_rings_comb["median"] %>% round(2),
    var_t0_rings_comb["median"] %>% round(2),
    cor_baseline_t0_summary_["median"] %>% round(2),
    resid_var["median"] %>% round(2)
  ),
  ci_lower = c(
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_F %>% round(2),
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_M %>% round(2),
    t0_sexF_summary_trans["ci_lower.2.5%"],
    t0_sexM_summary_trans["ci_lower.2.5%"],
    first_date_t0_SDd_summary["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_lower[1] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_lower[2] %>% round(2),
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    cor_baseline_t0_summary_["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    resid_var["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2)
  ),
  ci_upper = c(
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_F %>% round(2),
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_M %>% round(2),
    t0_sexF_summary_trans["ci_upper.97.5%"],
    t0_sexM_summary_trans["ci_upper.97.5%"],
    first_date_t0_SDd_summary["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_upper[1] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_upper[2] %>% round(2),
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    cor_baseline_t0_summary_["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    resid_var["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2)
  )
) %>%
  mutate(median_CI = paste0(mean, " [", ci_lower, ", ", ci_upper, "]"))
```

#### sex-specific phenology model: last nest lay date
```{r}
# ================================
# 2. Extract posterior draws
# ================================
post <- as_draws_df(sex_last_nest_breed_mod$fitted_model)

# ----------------
# Standardized sex effect on baseline
resid_sd <- sd(sex_last_nest_breed_mod$data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
sex_baseline_std <- post$b_baseline_sexM / resid_sd
sex_t0_std <- post$b_t0_sexM / resid_sd

lay_sd <- round(sd(sex_last_nest_breed_mod$data$last_nest_first_obs_J_snum, na.rm = TRUE))

# ----------------
# Back-transform last_nest_first_obs_J_snum effect to "per 10 days"
# Multiply by mean_date_sd to go from standardized units to days
# Then multiply by 10 to express per 10 days

# last_nest_baseline_SDd <- post$b_baseline_last_nest_first_obs_J_snum * mean_date_sd * lay_sd
last_nest_t0_SDd       <- post$b_t0_last_nest_first_obs_J_snum * lay_sd

# Posterior summaries
# last_nest_baseline_SDd_summary <- summarize_posterior(last_nest_baseline_SDd)
last_nest_t0_SDd_summary       <- summarize_posterior(last_nest_t0_SDd)

# ----------------
# Compute sex-specific baseline medians and 95% CI
baseline_sex_summary <- post %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_F = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_M = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    median_F     = median(baseline_F),
    ci_lower_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.025),
    ci_upper_F   = quantile(baseline_F, 0.975),
    median_M     = median(baseline_M),
    ci_lower_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.025),
    ci_upper_M   = quantile(baseline_M, 0.975)
  )

# ================================
# 3. Back-transform t0 and baseline
# ================================
date_scaling <- sex_last_nest_breed_mod$data %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_J = mean(date_J),
      sd_J   = sd(date_J),
      n      = dplyr::n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

# Weighted mean SD across seasons (recommended)
mean_date_sd   <- with(date_scaling, weighted.mean(sd_J, w = n))
mean_date_mean <- with(date_scaling, weighted.mean(mean_J, w = n))

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # sex-specific baseline
    baseline_sexF = b_baseline_Intercept,
    baseline_sexM = b_baseline_Intercept + b_baseline_sexM,

    # t0 is NOT sex-specific in this model
    t0_sexF_scaled = b_t0_Intercept,
    t0_sexM_scaled = b_t0_Intercept + b_t0_sexM,
    t0_sexF = t0_sexF_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean,
    t0_sexM = t0_sexM_scaled * mean_date_sd + mean_date_mean
  )

# ================================
# 4. Posterior summary helper
# ================================
summarize_posterior <- function(x) {
  c(
    median   = median(x),
    ci_lower = quantile(x, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(x, 0.975)
  )
}

# ----------------
# Summarize key fixed effects
std_sex_baseline_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_baseline_std)
std_sex_t0_summary <- summarize_posterior(sex_t0_std)
t0_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$t0_sexF)
t0_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$t0_sexM)
baseline_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$baseline_sexF)
baseline_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$baseline_sexM)

# back-transformed t0 predictions (real dates)
t0_sexF_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_sexF_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01")) %>% 
  format(., "%b-%d")

t0_sexM_summary_trans <- 
  as.Date(t0_sexM_summary - 181 - 1, origin = paste0("2021", "-01-01")) %>% 
  format(., "%b-%d")

t0_sexM_summary_trans
t0_sexF_summary_trans

# ================================
# 5. Interpret k: peak rate of change in days·score⁻¹
# ================================
date_sd <- mean_date_sd

post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    # Peak rate in scores·day⁻¹
    rate_scores_day_sexF = ((baseline_sexF - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    rate_scores_day_sexM = ((baseline_sexM - 1) * b_k_Intercept) / (4 * date_sd),
    # Convert to days·score⁻¹
    rate_days_score_sexF = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexF,
    rate_days_score_sexM = 1 / rate_scores_day_sexM
  )

rate_sexF_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexF)
rate_sexM_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$rate_days_score_sexM)

# ================================
# 6. Bayesian R²
# ================================
r2_df <- r2_bayes(sex_last_nest_breed_mod$fitted_model) %>% as.data.frame()

r2_summary <- tibble(
  type     = c("Marginal R²", "Conditional R²"),
  mean     = c(r2_df$R2[2], r2_df$R2[1]),
  ci_lower = c(r2_df$CI_low[2], r2_df$CI_low[1]),
  ci_upper = c(r2_df$CI_high[2], r2_df$CI_high[1])
)

# ================================
# 7. Variance components & repeatabilities
# ================================
post <- post %>%
  mutate(
    var_baseline_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2,
    var_t0_indiv =
      sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2 +
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2,
    R_baseline = var_baseline_indiv / (var_baseline_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_t0       = var_t0_indiv / (var_t0_indiv + sigma^2),
    R_baseline_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__baseline_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2),
    R_t0_within_season =
      `sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 /
      (`sd_rings_comb:season__t0_Intercept`^2 + sigma^2)
  )

R_baseline_summary               <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline)
R_t0_summary                     <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0)
R_baseline_within_season_summary <- summarize_posterior(post$R_baseline_within_season)
R_t0_within_season_summary       <- summarize_posterior(post$R_t0_within_season)

# ================================
# 8. Variance component table
# ================================
var_baseline_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept^2
)

var_t0_rings_comb <- summarize_posterior(
  post$sd_rings_comb__t0_Intercept^2
)

resid_var <- summarize_posterior(post$sigma^2)

# ================================
# 11. Posterior correlation between baseline and t0
# ================================
cor_baseline_t0_summary_ <- summarize_posterior(post$cor_rings_comb__baseline_Intercept__t0_Intercept)

### summary table 
sex_last_nest_summary_table <- tibble(
  parameter = c(
    "Female median alternate plumage (beta)",
    "Male median alternate plumage (beta)",
    "Female pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Male pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Effect of a 1-month shift in last nest lay date on moult mid-point date",
    "Female moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "Male moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "Marginal R²",
    "Conditional R²",
    "Random effect variance (baseline)",
    "Random effect variance (t0)",
    "Correlation between beta and t0",
    "Residual variance"
  ),
  type = c(
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Posterior estimates (state-specific means)",
    "Model-level goodness-of-fit",
    "Model-level goodness-of-fit",
    "Between-individual variance (random effects)",
    "Between-individual variance (random effects)",
    "Random-effects correlation",
    "Residual (within-observation) variance"
  ),
  parameter_ = c(
    "Full alternate plumage score (beta)",
    "Full alternate plumage score (beta)",
    "Pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Pre-basic moult mid-point date (t0)",
    "Breeding phenology",
    "Moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "Moult rate (days·score⁻¹, k)",
    "",
    "",
    "",
    "",
    "",
    ""
  ),
  mean = c(
    baseline_sex_summary$median_F %>% round(2),
    baseline_sex_summary$median_M %>% round(2),
    t0_sexF_summary_trans["median"],
    t0_sexM_summary_trans["median"],
    last_nest_t0_SDd_summary["median"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexF_summary["median"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexM_summary["median"] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$mean[1] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$mean[2] %>% round(2),
    var_baseline_rings_comb["median"] %>% round(2),
    var_t0_rings_comb["median"] %>% round(2),
    cor_baseline_t0_summary_["median"] %>% round(2),
    resid_var["median"] %>% round(2)
  ),
  ci_lower = c(
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_F %>% round(2),
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_lower_M %>% round(2),
    t0_sexF_summary_trans["ci_lower.2.5%"],
    t0_sexM_summary_trans["ci_lower.2.5%"],
    last_nest_t0_SDd_summary["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_lower[1] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_lower[2] %>% round(2),
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    cor_baseline_t0_summary_["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2),
    resid_var["ci_lower.2.5%"] %>% round(2)
  ),
  ci_upper = c(
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_F %>% round(2),
    baseline_sex_summary$ci_upper_M %>% round(2),
    t0_sexF_summary_trans["ci_upper.97.5%"],
    t0_sexM_summary_trans["ci_upper.97.5%"],
    last_nest_t0_SDd_summary["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexF_summary["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    rate_sexM_summary["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_upper[1] %>% round(2),
    r2_summary$ci_upper[2] %>% round(2),
    var_baseline_rings_comb["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    var_t0_rings_comb["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    cor_baseline_t0_summary_["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2),
    resid_var["ci_upper.97.5%"] %>% round(2)
  )
) %>%
  mutate(median_CI = paste0(mean, " [", ci_lower, ", ", ci_upper, "]"))

sex_last_nest_summary_table

# ================================
# 9. Posterior distributions of fixed effects
# ================================
posterior_fixed <- sex_last_nest_breed_mod$fitted_model %>%
  spread_draws(
    b_baseline_Intercept,
    b_baseline_sexM,
    b_baseline_last_nest_first_obs_J_snum,
    b_t0_Intercept,
    b_t0_sexM,
    b_t0_last_nest_first_obs_J_snum,
    b_k_Intercept
  )

posterior_long <- posterior_fixed %>%
  select(-c(.chain, .iteration, .draw)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "estimate")

ggplot(posterior_long, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),
               fill = "skyblue",
               color = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior distributions of fixed effects",
    x = "Estimate",
    y = "Parameter"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# # ================================
# # 10. Access key derived quantities
# # ================================
# post$baseline_sexF
# post$baseline_sexM
# post$t0
# post$rate_days_score_sexF
# post$rate_days_score_sexM

# ================================
# 12. Posterior predictive checks
# ================================
# Plot observed vs simulated data
pp_check(sex_last_nest_breed_mod$fitted_model, ndraws = 100)

# ================================
# 13. R-hat checks for convergence
# ================================
rhat_values <- posterior::rhat(sex_last_nest_breed_mod$fitted_model)
summary(rhat_values)  # All should be ~1 for good convergence
```
